symptom: ACI 每次启动/重启后出站流量的公网 IP 地址都会变化，客户无法将其加入防火墙白名单进行固定访问
rootCause: ACI 默认行为——容器组每次启动都会分配新的公网 IP，平台不保证出站 IP 固定
solution: 在 VNET 私有网络模式部署 ACI，并在该 VNET 的子网上关联 NAT Gateway（配置静态公网 IP），所有出站流量将通过 NAT Gateway 的固定 IP 出去；操作步骤：1) 创建 ACI（Private networking），2) 在 Portal 创建 NAT Gateway 并附加公网 IP，3) 在 NAT Gateway 的 Subnet 选项卡选择 ACI 所在子网
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/How To/Fixed IP Address for Outbound Connectivity in ACI through NAT Gateway

symptom: ACI 容器连接（exec/Portal connect）在长时间无用户交互后自动超时断开，即使有长时间运行的进程也会被踢出
rootCause: ACI 对无交互连接有超时机制，空闲连接会被平台关闭
solution: 在容器内运行 `nohup tail -f /dev/null` 保持连接活跃；需要恢复交互时按 Ctrl+C 结束该命令。部署示例：az container create 时加 --command-line "tail -f /dev/null" 防止容器退出
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/How To/Keep a connection to container group alive

symptom: 删除 ACI 容器组后，用相同 DNS 标签重建新容器组，FQDN 并未复用旧标签，DNS label reuse 不生效
rootCause: 重建容器组时未指定 DNS reuse policy；仅使用相同 DNS 标签不足以触发复用，必须同时在新容器组上明确指定相同的 dnsNameLabelReusePolicy（如 resourceGroupReuse）
solution: 在重建 ACI 容器组时，在 DNS label 设置的同时必须选择（或在 ARM/CLI 中指定）相同的 DNS Reuse Policy（如 ResourceGroupReuse）。5种策略：Unsecure / TenantReuse / SubscriptionReuse / ResourceGroupReuse / NoReuse，默认为 NoReuse
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/How To/Reusing DNS labels

symptom: VNet 中部署的 ACI 容器组重启或停止/启动后，私有 IP 地址发生变化，客户 DNS 记录仍指向旧 IP，导致通过 DNS 访问容器组失败（ping/连接超时）
rootCause: ACI 不支持静态私有 IP 地址，容器组重启后 IP 可能变化；现有 DNS 记录未能自动随 IP 变更更新
solution: 使用 Init Container 方案自动化 DNS 更新：参考 MS Learn 模块 https://learn.microsoft.com/learn/modules/secure-apps-azure-container-instances-sidecar/6-deploy-with-init-container ；Init Container 在容器组每次启动时运行，自动将新 IP 更新到 DNS 记录。注意：仍会有重启期间的短暂停机，但 DNS 记录会在启动后自动同步
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/How To/Sync Container Group Private IP Changes With Customer DNS records

symptom: ACI container group 创建后长时间停留在 Created 状态，镜像未能快速拉取
rootCause: ACI 不永久缓存镜像，大型镜像在未缓存时从 Registry 拉取需要较长时间。长拉取时间对于大型未缓存镜像属于预期行为。
solution: 1. 运行 `az container show` 查看 events 中 Pulling/Pulled 时间差，确认是镜像拉取耗时。2. 如果需要更快拉取，参考 https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/container-instances/2021-09-01/location/list-cached-images 使用 ACI 已缓存镜像。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/ACI Long Image Pull times

symptom: ACI Confidential Container 部署失败：Rego compilation failed / rego_parse_error: non-terminated string 或 unexpected assign token
rootCause: CCE Policy（Confidential Compute Enforcement Policy）包含额外字符、格式无效，或使用了错误语法导致 Rego 编译失败。
solution: 解码 CCE Policy 并验证内容格式和语法。快速修复：使用 confcom 工具重新生成 CCE Policy，重新部署。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/Confidential Containers

symptom: ACI Confidential Container 部署失败：mount_device not allowed by policy / deviceHash not found
rootCause: 本地 Docker 缓存中的镜像层与 CCE Policy 生成时使用的层不一致。镜像有新版本但本地缓存了旧版，confcom 工具在生成策略时跳过了 pull，使用了本地旧层的 hash，导致部署时 hash 不匹配。
solution:
1. 清理本地镜像缓存：`docker rmi <image_name>:<tag>` 或 `docker rmi $(docker images -a -q)` 
2. 使用 `docker inspect <image_name>:<tag>` 检查缺失的 hash 
3. 重新生成 CCE Policy（confcom 工具会显示警告） 
4. 重新部署。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/Confidential Containers

symptom: ACI Confidential Container 部署失败：Container creation denied due to policy: create_container not allowed by policy
rootCause: CCE Policy 中缺少 `create_container := data.framework.create_container` 配置，策略不允许容器创建操作。
solution: 解码 CCE Policy，验证 `create_container := data.framework.create_container` 配置是否存在。快速修复：使用 confcom 工具重新生成 CCE Policy。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/Confidential Containers

symptom: ACI Confidential Container 部署失败：mount_overlay not allowed by policy / framework_svn is ahead of the current svn (e.g. 1.1.0 > 0.1.0)
rootCause: 客户的 CCE Policy 强制要求更新的 framework svn，但当前平台尚未支持该版本。
solution: 快速修复：回退到较旧的 framework svn，重新生成 CCE Policy 后重新部署。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/Confidential Containers

symptom: ACI Confidential Container 部署后容器退出码 139（Segmentation fault）
rootCause: 已知问题：使用 Ubuntu 22.04 作为基础镜像的容器在 Confidential Containers 环境中会触发 Segmentation fault（exit code 139）。
solution: 建议客户切换为非 Ubuntu 22.04 的基础镜像（例如 Ubuntu 20.04 或其他发行版）重新构建镜像后部署。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/Confidential Containers

symptom: ACI Confidential Container 容器持续重启但无错误日志，Liveness Probe 配置的命令未在 CCE Policy 中
rootCause: CCE Policy 中没有包含 Liveness Probe 的命令，容器组成功 provision 但探针命令被策略拒绝，导致容器不断重启。
solution: 解码 CCE Policy，验证 Liveness Probe 命令是否在 Policy 中。快速修复：重新生成 CCE Policy（confcom 工具会将 liveness probe 命令包含在内），重新部署。如仍有问题，向 ACI Team 开 IcM。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/Confidential Containers

symptom: ACI container group 间歇性重启，容器日志无错误，无明显用户操作触发
rootCause: 平台事件（Platform Event）触发了 REPAIRDEPLOYMENTS 操作。operationName 为 `POST/ADMIN/LOCATIONS/CLUSTERS/REPAIRDEPLOYMENTS` 且 clientApplicationId 为 null 时，确认为平台事件导致，属于预期行为。
solution:
1. 收集 Resource ID 和 UTC 时间 
2. 用 Kusto 查询 accprod.SubscriptionDeployments 找到 start 操作的 correlationId 
3. 用 correlationId 查 HttpIncomingRequests，检查 operationName 
4. 若 operationName = REPAIRDEPLOYMENTS 且 clientApplicationId = null → 平台事件，预期行为 
5. 向客户发送公开文档：https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/container-instances/container-instances-troubleshooting#container-had-an-isolated-restart-without-explicit-user-input
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/Container Group restart due to Platform events

symptom: ACI 使用 Managed Identity 拉取 ACR 镜像时报错：InvalidImageRegistryCredentialType — identity in imageRegistryCredentials is not supported
rootCause: 客户使用的 ACI API 版本低于 2021-07-01，该版本不支持 Managed Identity 认证方式。
solution: 要求客户升级 ARM Template 使用的 API 版本至 2021-07-01 或更新版本，参考：https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/container-instances/container-instances-reference-yaml
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/Managed Identity Auth Image Pulls

symptom: ACI 使用 Managed Identity 拉取 ACR 镜像时报错：AmbiguousImageRegistryCredentialType / InvalidImageRegistryIdentity / Required property 'server' not found
rootCause: Container Group ARM Template 定义不规范：imageRegistryCredentials 中同时设置了 username 和 identity（只能设置其一），或 identity 不在 ContainerGroupIdentity 列表中，或缺少必填的 server 字段。
solution: 检查 ARM Template：1. imageRegistryCredentials 中 username 和 identity 只能设置其一 2. ContainerGroupIdentity 中必须包含 imageRegistryCredentials 所引用的 managed identity 3. imageRegistryCredentials 必须包含 server 字段。参考文档：https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/templates/microsoft.containerinstance/containergroups
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/Managed Identity Auth Image Pulls

symptom: ACI 使用 Managed Identity 拉取 ACR 私有镜像时报错：InaccessibleImage — image is not accessible
rootCause: Managed Identity 实例未被授予 ACR 的 Pull 权限（AcrPull RBAC role）。
solution:
1. 为 Managed Identity 分配 ACR AcrPull 角色，参考：https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/container-registry/container-registry-tasks-authentication-managed-identity#3-grant-the-identity-permissions-to-access-other-azure-resources 
2. 检查 ACR 是否启用了 Trusted Services（通过 Resource Explorer 确认 access 设置）
3. 若以上均正常，通过 IcM 模板提交：https://portal.microsofticm.com/imp/v3/incidents/create?tmpl=g2E3T2
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/Managed Identity Auth Image Pulls

symptom: Customer receives Azure Service Health notification about ACI security vulnerability; concerned about potential container/tenant boundary escape; asking if their data was compromised or if attackers accessed other tenants' containers
rootCause: CVE-2019-5736 and CVE-2018-1002102 vulnerabilities in Kubernetes and container runtime. ACI was running on old Kubernetes-based stack susceptible to these CVEs. Security researchers from Palo Alto Networks (reported July 1, 2021) inferred internal ACI architecture and attacked ACI custom components. Vulnerability could allow user to escape container and tenant boundary.
solution: Microsoft deployed fix Aug 31, 2021. Current ACI: (1) runs each container in dedicated VM with hypervisor isolation, (2) uses custom Bridge Service for 'exec' commands instead of Kubernetes API server. Both mechanisms prevent CVE exploitation. Actions: validate if customer subscription was in impacted list; recommend rotating secrets/credentials used in ACI deployments; check access/audit logs of secret stores, storage accounts, databases for suspicious activity. No evidence of exploitation was found. Use 'Vulnerability Related Inquiries' Root Cause in ACI Root Cause Tree when closing ticket.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/Emerging and Known Issues/ACI Vulnerability

symptom: Unable to delete resource group, VNET, or subnet; error indicates ACI Service Association Link (acisal) is blocking deletion even after all container groups appear to be deleted
rootCause: ACI Service Association Link (acisal) remains attached to the network resource after container group deletion, preventing removal of the parent network resource
solution: Step 1: Try CSS/Cx mitigations from TSG: /Azure-Kubernetes-Service-Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG]-Cannot-delete-ACI-Vnet-or-Subnet-due-to-dangling-Service-Association-Link. Step 2: If all mitigations fail, the only way to unblock deletion is via ACIS Action NRP/DeleteServiceAssociationLink. This requires OaaS (https://aka.ms/oaas) and R2D (https://aka.ms/r2d) clearance (SafeFly). Only EEE ACI team is onboarded with these clearances — escalate to EEE ACI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/List of ACI issues where IcM is required

symptom: Unable to delete resource group, VNET, or subnet; Azure Support Center (ASC) shows lingering NICs in Network Profile; container groups themselves are already deleted and do not appear in ASC
rootCause: Orphan NICs remain in Network Profile after Container Group deletion (known as 'orphan NICs in Network Profile' issue)
solution: CSS validation: (1) confirm CGs are deleted via ASC, (2) confirm lingering NICs shown in Network Profile in ASC. For mitigation, must escalate to EEE ACI to use ACIS Action ACI/Remove Unused CNI. Only EEE ACI and PG have access to the TSG and required JIT clearance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/List of ACI issues where IcM is required

symptom: Cannot delete Container Group, returns Internal Error; ASI issue detectors show 'failed_stop_deployment_errors' on both CG page and caas page detectors; related to Emerging Issue 97623
rootCause: Internal ACI backend error preventing Container Group deletion; Service Fabric unable to stop the deployment
solution: CSS validates issue via ASI detectors (failed_stop_deployment_errors in both CG and caas pages). Must escalate to EEE/PG: use ACIS Action ACI/Delete Container Group Skip Decryption. Only EEE ACI and PG have access to TSG and required JIT clearance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/List of ACI issues where IcM is required

symptom: Cannot delete Container Group; ASI shows Failed Delete Operations detector triggered; RP Error Kusto table shows deletion errors related to managed identity or Key Vault dependency; related to Emerging Issue 68347
rootCause: Container Group has dependency on a managed identity or Key Vault that has been deleted or become inaccessible, blocking CG deletion from backend
solution: Validate with Kusto query on accprod.Errors: filter by correlationId and operationName containing 'delete' to confirm error pattern. Also check ASI Failed Delete Operations detector. Must escalate to EEE: use ACIS Action ACI/Delete Container Group Skip Decryption. Only EEE ACI and PG have required JIT clearance. Kusto query: cluster('accprod').database('accprod').Errors | where PreciseTimeStamp between (queryFrom..queryTo) | where correlationId == '<correlationId>' | where operationName has 'delete' | project PreciseTimeStamp, correlationId, operationName, message, exception | sort by PreciseTimeStamp asc
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/List of ACI issues where IcM is required

symptom: ACI Container Group continues running or remains accessible after it should have been stopped; customer reports ghost container IP still active; unexpected running caas deployment visible in Kusto SubscriptionDeployments
rootCause: Service Fabric (SF) fails to deactivate a Code Package due to node issues; old caas deployment persists unexpectedly
solution: Use Kusto SubscriptionDeployments query to find caas matching ghost IP and timestamp. Query: SubscriptionDeployments | where PreciseTimeStamp between (queryFrom..queryTo) and subscriptionId =~ querySubscriptionId and resourceGroup =~ queryResourceGroup and containerGroup =~ queryContainerGroupName | where ipAddress != '' and ipAddress != '<null>' | project PreciseTimeStamp, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, containerGroup, clusterDeploymentName, ipAddress, location, TaskName | sort by PreciseTimeStamp asc. If customer wants to stop CG: escalate to EEE for ACI/Stop Container Group ACIS action (elevated JIT required). If newer caas is running expectedly, transfer to PG/SF PG for mitigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/List of ACI issues where IcM is required

symptom: ACI Container Group deployment fails with insufficient capacity error in a specific region; customer cannot provision new container groups
rootCause: Insufficient platform capacity in the region/cluster pool for requested ACI SKU or configuration
solution: Validate via ASI Insufficient Capacity detector on CG page. Attempt to reproduce the deployment to confirm same error. Ask customer to try deployment in another region to determine scope. After EEE validates capacity dashboards, transfer issue from EEE to PG for capacity mitigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/List of ACI issues where IcM is required

symptom: Container Group logs not appearing in Log Analytics Workspace, or Azure Monitor metrics not showing for ACI resource
rootCause: For missing metrics: Geneva Metric definition issues in ACI's shoebox account. For missing logs: Log Uploader component failures in SF cluster
solution: Use ASI to validate: (1) Metrics tab on CG page for missing metrics gaps; (2) Log Uploader Events tab on caas page for missing log errors (also use Kusto query from Kusto-Repo-ACI.md#loganalyticsuploader-events). For missing metrics: EEE checks Geneva Metric definitions, then works with PG to fix. For missing logs: EEE inspects related components in SF cluster, ultimately transfers to PG for fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/List of ACI issues where IcM is required

solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-aci-automate-sync-ip-private-dns-zone.md

symptom: ACI deployed on VNet does not have a static IP; Application Gateway backend pool uses the dynamic ACI IP which changes on restart; need to automatically sync AppGw backend pool when ACI restarts
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-aci-automate-sync-ip-appgw-backend-pool.md

symptom: ACI container continues to be billed after customer stops or deletes the container group; billing persists unexpectedly
rootCause: Leaked deployment in ACI backend (SubscriptionDeploymentIndex not cleaned up); billing job continues reporting usage for lingering cluster records
solution: Have customer create a new container group with the same resourceId in the same region to force backend cleanup. Verify with KQL (SubscriptionDeployments + BillingUsageEvents) that billing stops. If refund needed, collect billing table data and escalate to Commerce team via CSS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] ACI billing related issues

symptom: ACI creation fails with error: '(RegistryErrorResponse) An error response is received from the docker registry index.docker.io. Please retry later.' or TOOMANYREQUESTS 429 from Docker Hub
rootCause: Docker Hub rate limiting. The Microsoft-Docker allowlist agreement that allowed unlimited anonymous pulls from Azure IPs expired on June 30, 2024. Anonymous pulls are now throttled (free: 100/6h unauthenticated; Docker free account: 200/6h).
solution: 1) Ask customer to create a Docker account and authenticate pulls. 2) Recommend using ACR Artifact Cache with authenticated cache rule to cache Docker Hub images inside ACR. 3) If 200 pulls insufficient, recommend paid Docker Subscription (5000/24h). Confirm error via Kusto: check Traces for 'TOOMANYREQUESTS' with customer's correlationId.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] ACI_Docker_RateLimit

symptom: Azure Image Builder (AIB) container in ACI hangs and fails to start during VHD image build; error: FailedMountAzureFileVolume, exit code 255; image build times out
rootCause: NSG rule blocking traffic to the storage account needed to mount the Azure File Volume. An Azure Policy modified the NSG on creation by adding a deny-all rule that is not part of AIB's default NSG configuration.
solution: Method 1: Add a policy exemption to the AIB resource group to prevent the deny-all NSG rule from being added. Verify storage account traffic using test container. Method 2: Use BYOVNET with an existing VNet configured to allow required storage traffic (see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/linux/image-builder-networking#deploy-using-an-existing-vnet). Use Kusto ACI query to track CG deployed by VM Image Builder; add resource lock to resource group to preserve resources for investigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Azure Image Builder ACI Troubleshooting

symptom: Deletion of ACI subnet or VNet fails even after all container groups are removed; error: 'Subnet requires any of the following delegations [Microsoft.ContainerInstance/containerGroups] to reference service association link .../serviceAssociationLinks/acisal'
rootCause: Dangling Service Association Link (SAL) 'acisal' remains on the subnet after ACI deletion. Setting subnet delegation to 'None' does not work while acisal is present.
solution: 1) Verify all deployments are deleted (check ASI or use SubscriptionDeployments KQL query). 2) Delete SAL via CLI: `az resource delete --ids /subscriptions/.../serviceAssociationLinks/acisal --api-version 2018-10-01`, then retry subnet deletion. 3) If steps fail or customer needs to remove delegation without deleting subnet, open IcM to ACI team for SAL deletion via ACIS action.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Cannot delete ACI Vnet or Subnet due to dangling Service Association Link

symptom: Cannot connect to ACI container from Azure Portal when container is deployed in BYOVNET with a Firewall; error: 'The following web socket error occurred: Web socket is closed or could not be opened.'
rootCause: TCP port 19390 is required for the portal 'Connect to Container' feature in the BYOVNET scenario, but this requirement is not documented. Firewall rules block traffic on this port.
solution: Allow ingress to TCP port 19390 in the Firewall for all public client IPs from which Azure Portal will be accessed. This is a service port used by ACI for portal connectivity in BYOVNET.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Connect to Container requirements in BYOVNET

symptom: ACI container application-level issues: container restarts, application errors, no long-running process, need to debug container behavior
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-container-debug-tools.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Container Debug Tools

symptom: BYOVNET ACI container group deployment hangs in 'Waiting' status and fails after 30-minute timeout; error: 'Subscription deployment didn't reach a successful provisioning state after 00:30:00'; SF logs show 'Failed to add NC ... node has reached maximum capacity'
rootCause: Network Container (NC) Leak in Service Fabric infrastructure: a node in the execution cluster has reached its maximum NC capacity (known SF bug), preventing new network container assignments.
solution: Retry the deployment (transient infrastructure issue). Reference emerging issue: 'ACI Network Container (NC) Leak'. No customer-side workaround; retry should unblock. Check SF execution cluster logs with Kusto SbzExecSFEvent query filtering for 'Failed to add NC' and 'has reached maximum capacity'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Container Group deployment hangs in Waiting status

symptom: BYOVNET ACI container group deployment hangs in 'Waiting' status, times out after 30 min; SF logs show E_FAIL 'There was an error during activation' with 'subnet capacity exceeded'
rootCause: The subnet in the Atlas execution cluster has exceeded its IP/capacity limit; Service Fabric cannot activate the code package due to subnet exhaustion.
solution: Investigate SF execution cluster logs (SbzExecSFEvent) for the specific caas name filtering on 'E_FAIL' and 'subnet capacity exceeded'. Escalate to ACI/Atlas PG team with the execution cluster ID, Atlas region, and SingleInstanceName for subnet capacity remediation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Container Group deployment hangs in Waiting status

symptom: ACI container group intermittently restarts unexpectedly; portal/Log Analytics shows 'Killing container with id ...' event; exit code 7147
rootCause: Service Fabric gracefully shut down the container by sending Ctrl+C signal (exit code 7147). Can be triggered by: user-initiated restart action, memory limit exceeded causing SF to cycle the container, or platform maintenance.
solution: 1) Check ARM logs for user-initiated restart actions around the kill timestamp using ARM log query. 2) Check container memory usage with Kusto memory query to rule out OOM. 3) Exit code 7147 with UnexpectedTermination=false and 'Deactivating as part of request from Activator' in SF logs = user-triggered, expected behavior. If memory exceeded: advise customer to increase memory limit.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Container killing event interruptions unexpected stops

symptom: ACI container group unexpectedly restarts; portal shows 'Killing container' event; exit code 7148
rootCause: Service Fabric forcefully terminated the container process because it did not respond to the Ctrl+C signal in a timely manner (exit code 7148). Usually indicates the customer application is unresponsive or deadlocked.
solution: Work with customer to identify the root cause of application unresponsiveness. If customer uses a Microsoft SDK (e.g., .NET Framework), engage Dev POD support team. Review application logs for deadlocks, resource exhaustion, or unhandled exceptions at the time of the kill event.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Container killing event interruptions unexpected stops

symptom: ACI container group restarts with non-zero exit code (not 7147 or 7148); container killing event observed
rootCause: Non-zero exit code (other than 7147/7148) indicates the customer application itself crashed or exited with an error, not a Service Fabric-initiated termination.
solution: Collect container exit code from SF execution cluster 'Hosting' logs (SbzExecSFEvent). Advise customer to review application logs and error handling. If using Microsoft SDK (.NET Framework etc.), engage Dev POD support team for application-level debugging.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Container killing event interruptions unexpected stops

symptom: ACI container group IP address changes after an unexpected restart (SF restart), causing connectivity disruption to applications that connect to the container group by IP
rootCause: ACI does not support static IP by default; every SF-level restart may assign a new IP address to the container group.
solution: Short term: (1) Use Application Gateway with backend pool pointing to the ACI delegated subnet. (2) Request a DNS name for the container group and use DNS name instead of IP for communication. Long term: ACI static IP feature (in preview as of 2023).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Container killing event interruptions unexpected stops

symptom: ACI container taking long time to provision or failing to provision; deployment does not reach running state
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-container-taking-long-time-to-provision-failing.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Container taking long time to provision failing

symptom: Public ACI deployment fails with 'Encountered an internal server error' when trying to reuse a DNS label; deployment correlation traceable to PUT/FRONTEND/SUBSCRIPTIONS/PROVIDERS/MICROSOFT.NETWORK/DNSNAMERESERVATIONS failure
rootCause: DNS reservation RP bug: throws 'Guid should contain 32 digits with 4 dashes' exception under certain conditions when processing DNS label reservation. Exact trigger conditions unclear (Bug 20980860).
solution: 1) Use latest ACI API version. 2) Ensure no capital letters in DNS label value. 3) Use a different DNS label (workaround). 4) If must reuse same DNS label: open IcM to ACI team to involve Azure DNS Name Reservation team for manual cleanup of the existing label.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] DNS Name Reservation

symptom: ACI container group in VNet goes into restart loop; Private Link or Private DNS resolves Azure service endpoint (e.g., Key Vault) to its public IP instead of private IP. Container logs show HTTP 403 ForbiddenByFirewall with public client address from the ACI container.
rootCause: Race condition bug in NMAgent (Azure networking service) causes ACI to resolve the service endpoint's public IP instead of private IP. Stale records in DnsForwarder are not cleaned up, causing persistent incorrect DNS resolution for Private Link endpoints.
solution: 1. Query Kusto SubscriptionDeployments with containerGroup resourceURI to get node and clusterId. 2. Query ClusterHealth with clusterId to get infra VM subscription ID from manifest. 3. Create IcM to Cloudnet\DNS Recursive team with infra VM subscription ID and VM name. 4. Link IcM to parent IcM 226910502. 5. Ask Cloudnet team to clean up stale DnsForwarder records. New deployments should resume normal functionality after cleanup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/Private Link or Private DNS are resolving to azure resource's Public IP instead of private IP

symptom: Error 'Subnet xxx is in use by .../Microsoft.Network/networkProfiles/aci-network-profile-xxx/.../ipconfigprofile and cannot be deleted' when attempting to delete subnet or VNet previously used by ACI container groups.
rootCause: The network profile created by ACI during container group creation is not properly cleaned up when the container group is deleted, leaving a dangling network profile resource that holds a reference to the subnet and blocks its deletion.
solution: Workaround 1 (Portal): Show hidden types in Resource Group → select and delete the Network Profile for the ACI CG → retry subnet/VNet delete. Workaround 2 (CLI): Get profile ID via 'az network vnet subnet show --query ipConfigurationProfiles[].id', then 'az network profile delete --ids $NetworkProfile --yes', then delete subnet/VNet. Workaround 3: If above fail, update network profile's containerNetworkInterfaceConfigurations to empty list via 'az resource update --ids $NETWORK_PROFILE_ID --set properties.containerNetworkInterfaceConfigurations=[]' then delete. If all fail, file IcM to Azure Container Instance team for manual cleanup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/Unable to delete ACI Subnet or VNet Subnet is in use

symptom: ACI container instance fails to start with DeploymentTimeout after 30 minutes. Container logs show 'Existing volume mount key not found. Mount volume.' followed by failed mount operations. nslookup resolves Azure Files storage account to a public IP.
rootCause: Firewall rules, NSG policies, or VNet restrictions block outbound network access from the container group to the Azure Files storage endpoint (port 445/SMB), preventing the container from mounting the required Azure File Share volume.
solution: Check and update firewall rules and NSG rules to allow outbound access from the container group's subnet to the Azure Files storage account endpoint. As a workaround to confirm the root cause, redeploy the container group in a less-restricted VNet and verify successful startup, then apply appropriate firewall exceptions to the original VNet.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] ACI Container Instance Failing To Start because Mount Error

symptom: ACI Spot Priority container group deployment fails with HTTP 400 error code 'SpotPriorityContainerGroupNotSupportedForInboundConnectivity'.
rootCause: Spot Priority container groups do not support inbound connectivity. Network-related properties (e.g., public IP address, exposed ports for inbound) are incompatible with the Spot Priority deployment model.
solution: Remove all inbound network-related properties from the container group request (e.g., ipAddress type Public, inbound port rules) and redeploy with Spot Priority.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] ACI Spot Containers Customer Errors

symptom: ACI Spot Priority container group deployment fails with HTTP 400 error 'SpotPriorityContainerGroupNotSupportedInSku' for a non-Standard SKU.
rootCause: Spot Priority container groups only support Standard SKU. Dedicated or other SKUs are not supported for Spot deployments.
solution: Change the container group SKU to Standard in the deployment request. Only Standard SKU is supported for ACI Spot Priority.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] ACI Spot Containers Customer Errors

symptom: ACI Spot Priority container group deployment fails with HTTP 400 error 'SpotPriorityContainerGroupWithGPUResourcesNotSupported' when GPU resources are requested.
rootCause: GPU resources are not supported for ACI Spot Priority container groups. Spot containers cannot request nvidia.com/gpu or other GPU resource types.
solution: Remove GPU resource requests from all containers in the container group and redeploy as Spot. If GPU is required, use Regular Priority instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] ACI Spot Containers Customer Errors

symptom: ACI container group deployment fails with HTTP 400 error 'PriorityNotSpecified' — 'The Priority must be one of Regular,Spot for container group xyz'.
rootCause: The 'priority' field in the container group request body contains an invalid value (not 'Regular' or 'Spot').
solution: Set the 'priority' field in the container group request to either 'Regular' or 'Spot'. No other values are accepted.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] ACI Spot Containers Customer Errors

symptom: ACI Spot container group deployment fails with error 'ContainerGroupQuotaReached: container group quota StandardSpotCores exceeded in region X. Limit: X, Usage: Y, Requested: Z'.
rootCause: The subscription has reached its StandardSpotCores quota limit for ACI Spot containers in the target region. Default limits vary by subscription type: EA = 100, Default = 10, All others = 0.
solution: 1. Check capacity availability per region on Geneva Capacity dashboard. 2. Query SubscriptionPolicy Kusto table to check current quota by location. 3. Verify requested resources are within available limits per region. 4. Submit StandardSpotCores quota increase via ICM template (select 'StandardSpotCores' under Quota Type). Requests <=50 cores are auto-approved; >50 requires business justification and PG manual approval.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] ACI Spot Containers Quota Errors

symptom: ACI Spot container group enters 'Waiting' state after having been in 'Running' state. All containers in the container group are stuck waiting for a new node to be assigned, with no recovery.
rootCause: The underlying Spot VM hosting the container group was evicted due to Azure capacity adjustments. Spot VM evictions can happen at any time. The container group waits for relocation to a new node within the same or different cluster.
solution: This is expected behavior for Spot containers. Use the Spot ContainerGroup Evictions Kusto query (atlaslogscp.eastus/telemetry) with CRP ApiQosEvent_nonGet to confirm evictions (FabricCallback.OnVMScaleSetVMsEvicted.POST), track jump count and jump durations. See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-aci-spot-containers-evictions-kusto.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] ACI Spot Containers Waiting State Due To Evictions

symptom: ACI container group creation fails with HTTP 400 or 500. ACI internal logs show error 'MsiErrorResponseException' with message: error code 429 — 'Request is temporarily throttled because subscription X has issued too many requests. Retry after N seconds.'
rootCause: Multiple concurrent credential request calls to the Managed Identity (MSI) service violate the per-AAD-tenant/subscription/resource rate limits. ACI surfaces MSI's HTTP 429 throttling as HTTP 400 or 500 to the caller.
solution: Limit the rate of MSI credential requests per the Managed Identity service limits (see armwiki). Cache MSI credentials — they are valid for 45 days, so re-fetching on every request is unnecessary. Stagger resource provisioning to avoid burst MSI calls at deployment time.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] ACI Throttling at Managed Identity service

symptom: ACI Standby Pools deployment or management errors including: missing subnet join permissions, container image pull failures, ContainerGroupQuotaReached, ConfidentialContainerGroupSkuIsNotSupported, InvalidImageRegistryIdentity, VirtualNetworkNotFound.
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-aci-standby-pools-error-guide.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/Standby Pools

symptom: ACI container group has restart events, Service Fabric network errors, or requires deep RCA investigation across RP/Service Fabric/DNC layers.
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-aci-atlas-investigation-flow-kusto.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] ACI ATLAS Investigation Flow Kusto

symptom: ACI GPU container group deployment fails with timeout after 30 minutes or 'Internal Error'. Messages show '0/7 nodes are available: 6 Insufficient nvidia.com/gpu.' GPU CG deployment uses AKS Engine clusters with Nvidia driver; preview feature with no GA plan.
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-aci-gpu-deployment-failures.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] ACI GPU Deployment Failures

symptom: ACI Spot container group enters and remains in 'Waiting' state due to Spot VM evictions. Investigation requires tracing eviction events across CRP and Service Fabric Atlas clusters.
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-aci-spot-containers-evictions-kusto.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] ACI Spot Containers Waiting State Due To Evictions

symptom: ACI deployment fails with 'Resource type Microsoft.ContainerInstance/containerGroups quota exceeded in region. Limit: 0, Usage: 0, Requested: 1.' despite subscription showing available quota.
rootCause: Subscription or tenant has been flagged as fraud in ACI's internal fraud detection config. Multiple concurrent ACI deployments across regions using the same name from automation scripts commonly triggers FraudDetected in ACI RP traces (cluster accprod).
solution: 1. Confirm via Kusto: cluster('Accprod').database('accprod').Traces | where operationName contains 'Fraud'. 2. Check subscription/tenant ID in Compute-ACI-Config repo fraud-config.json. 3. Advise customer to issue one ACI deployment at a time (avoid concurrent). 4. Create ICM copy of 394233833 for HIT (Human Intelligence Team) to validate no actual fraudulent behavior.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Fraud Detected

symptom: ACI container group deployment fails with InaccessibleImage error: 'The image in container group is not accessible. Please check the image and registry credential.'
rootCause: Customer specified incorrect credentials (wrong password, spaces, or invalid characters) during container group creation.
solution: Ask customer to test credentials and confirm there are no spaces or invalid characters in the registry credential. Customer can switch to using a managed identity instead of service principal credentials.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] InaccessibleImage Error

symptom: ACI container group deployment fails with InaccessibleImage error when customer uses a service principal to access ACR that has network firewall rules enabled.
rootCause: ACI service calls to the container registry are blocked by ACR firewall rules. Service principals are not recognized as trusted Azure services by ACR.
solution: Customer must use a managed identity (not service principal) to allow ACI trusted service access to the ACR. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/container-registry/allow-access-trusted-services#about-trusted-services
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] InaccessibleImage Error

symptom: ACI container fails to obtain MSI (Managed Service Identity) token; AccessTokenRequestEvent shows unsuccessful=True token requests in Kusto.
rootCause: For BadRequest errors: subscription does not have access to the identity. May also be caused by ARM throttling limitations.
solution: 1. Check AccessTokenRequestEvent in Kusto for failure details (filter by Tenant/region, successful==False). 2. For BadRequest: verify subscription has been granted access to the target identity. 3. Check ARM limitations via: cluster('armprodgbl.eastus').database('ARMProd').Unionizer('Requests','HttpIncomingRequests') joined with Traces containing 'Limitation'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] MSITokenRequestFailure

symptom: Memory usage shown in Azure Portal for an ACI container group is lower than expected compared to values seen running 'free -h' inside the container. Observed in multi-container container groups.
rootCause: Portal displays the average memory usage of ALL containers in the Container Group at 1-minute granularity (not per-container). Presence of low-memory sidecar containers (e.g., postgres-exporter using ~20MB) brings the average well below the main container's actual usage.
solution: Use the dimension filter in Azure Portal to view memory metrics per individual container. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/container-instances/container-instances-monitor#get-metrics---azure-portal. Note: only Average aggregation is available for ACI memory metrics (Min/Max aggregations show no data per documentation).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Memory usage inside container does not match values in Portal

symptom: Need to trace ACI app provision or deletion lifecycle events (AllocationFailed, ApplicationRunning, ApplicationDeletionEnd, etc.) through Atlas/SeaBreeze RP.
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-aci-find-app-provision-deletion-flow.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Find app provision deletion flow

symptom: Need to find the underlying VM ID for a given ACI container group to investigate host-level issues.
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-aci-how-to-find-vm-id-for-aci-cg.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] How to find the VM id for a given ACI CG

symptom: ACI Atlas cluster creations are stuck and transitioning to DeletingState; need to identify dominant failure category across quota/KeyVault/ARM/SFRP/certificate errors at scale.
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-aci-investigate-stuck-cluster-creations.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Investigate stuck cluster creations

symptom: ACI SBZ (SeaBreeze) verifier test failures occurring; need to categorize failure types and diagnose RP-level deployment issues (networking, capacity, timeouts).
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-aci-investigating-sbz-verifier-failures.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Investigating SBZ Verifier failures

symptom: Need guidance on how to use ACI Kusto Helper and Atlas Kusto Helper Jarvis dashboards for end-to-end ACI investigation (K8s vs Atlas, SubscriptionDeployments, traces, ContainerD logs).
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-aci-kusto-helpers.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Kusto Helpers

symptom: ACI deployment returns ServiceUnavailable (409) error 'The requested resource is not available in the location at this moment' for CPU/memory/OS/vnet combination.
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-aci-service-unavailable-error.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Service Unavailable Error

symptom: Subnet deletion fails with error InUseSubnetCannotBeDeleted when ACI container groups were deployed into the subnet using VNet integration
rootCause: ACI creates a service association link (providers/Microsoft.ContainerInstance/serviceAssociationLinks/default) on the delegated subnet during container group deployment. This link persists even after the container group is deleted and blocks subnet deletion.
solution: Delete the service association link using: `az rest --method delete --uri https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{vnet}/subnets/{subnet}/providers/Microsoft.ContainerInstance/serviceAssociationLinks/default?api-version=2018-10-01`. Then retry the subnet deletion.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Subnet Deletion Error: InUseSubnetCannotBeDeleted

symptom: ACI container group deployment on Atlas backend fails with SubscriptionDeploymentUnhealthy task error in the operations log
rootCause: The underlying Atlas cluster is in an unhealthy state due to a cluster upgrade in progress or a bad cluster node assignment.
solution: Query SubscriptionDeployments + JobTraces/JobErrors in ACI Kusto Helper (Jarvis) using the containerGroup name and timeframe to identify the TriggerEvictions event. If cluster is upgrading, wait for upgrade completion. If cluster is bad, escalate to ACI PG for cluster remediation. Use clusterDeploymentName from SubscriptionDeployments to pivot into Atlas Kusto Helper for deeper investigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] SubscriptionDeploymentUnhealthy (Atlas deployment)

symptom: ACI container group logs not appearing in Log Analytics Workspace; ContainerInstanceLog_CL table is missing; SbzExecLogUploaderEvent shows 'Upload failed with Http 403'
rootCause: The Log Analytics Workspace key used by the container group is invalid or has been rotated/expired, causing authentication failure when ACI attempts to push logs.
solution: Verify the Log Analytics Workspace key configured on the container group is current. Retrieve the correct workspace key from the LAW portal and update the container group's diagnostic settings with the valid key.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Unable to send ACI logs to Log Analytics Workspace

symptom: ACI container group logs not appearing in Log Analytics Workspace when AMPLS (Azure Monitor Private Link Scope) is configured with public network access disabled
rootCause: ACI log integration does not support Azure Monitor Private Link Scope (AMPLS). When public network ingestion is disabled on the LAW, ACI container instances cannot communicate with the workspace to send logs.
solution: In the Log Analytics Workspace network settings, set 'Accept data ingestion and queries from public networks' to Yes. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/private-link-configure#configure-access-to-ampls-resources. Note: AMPLS is not a supported configuration for ACI log integration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Unable to send ACI logs to Log Analytics Workspace

symptom: ACI container group logs not appearing in Log Analytics Workspace; local authentication is disabled on the workspace (disableLocalAuth=true)
rootCause: When local authentication is disabled on a Log Analytics Workspace, ACI cannot authenticate to send logs because ACI log integration uses workspace key-based (local) authentication. The log ingestion fails silently.
solution: Re-enable local authentication on the Log Analytics Workspace: `az resource update --ids "/subscriptions/<sub>/resourceGroups/<rg>/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/<workspace>" --api-version "2021-06-01" --set properties.features.disableLocalAuth=false`. Verify current value first with `az resource show --ids <workspace-resource-id>`.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Unable to send ACI logs to Log Analytics Workspace

symptom: ACI container group deployment or runtime issue requires investigation using Kusto dashboards; need to determine if deployment is on K8 or Atlas backend and where to look for logs
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-aci-troubleshooting-basics.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/TSG/[TSG] Troubleshooting Basics

symptom: ACStor PersistentVolume mount point becomes read-only; kernel logs show 'blk_update_request: I/O error' on nvme device and 'XFS log I/O Error ... Shutting down filesystem'; app container gets 'cannot open directory: Input/output error'; database apps report volume in ReadOnly mode
rootCause: ACStor volume nexus has a remote replica that disconnected; when no healthy replicas remain in persistent store, the nexus retires the replica and the XFS filesystem shuts down, making the volume read-only
solution: 1. Port-forward api-rest: kubectl port-forward <xxx-api-rest-xxx> -n acstor 8081:8081; 2. Check nexus/replica status: curl -X GET 'http://127.0.0.1:8081/v0/volumes/<nexus-name>' - verify .state.status and .state.target.state are 'Online'; 3. Scale down app pod: kubectl scale deploy/sts -n <ns> --replicas=<n-1>; 4. Scale back up to reconnect replica
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/ACStor PersistentVolumes becoming ReadOnly

symptom: ACStor PVC creation fails with 'Operation failed due to insufficient resources: Not enough suitable pools available' when describing pvc
rootCause: Requested storage size exceeds available capacity in the ACStor storage pools; check capacity with 'kubectl get sp -n acstor'
solution: Add a new StoragePool to support the requested volume; for AzureDisk, expanding the existing DiskPool also works and is supported in GA
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Creating Or Deleting PersistentVolumes issues

symptom: ACStor snapshot creation fails with 'Operation failed due to insufficient resources: Not enough suitable pools available' when describing VolumeSnapshot
rootCause: Requested storage size for snapshot exceeds available capacity in ACStor storage pools
solution: Delete some existing snapshots or volumes to free capacity; check available capacity with 'kubectl get sp -n acstor'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Creating Or Deleting Snapshots Issues

symptom: ACStor snapshot creation fails with 'Cannot create a snapshot for volume %s because it would exceed the maximum limit of 10 snapshots' when describing VolumeSnapshot
rootCause: ACStor enforces a hard limit of maximum 10 snapshots per volume
solution: Delete some existing snapshots for that volume to fall below the 10-snapshot limit before creating new ones
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Creating Or Deleting Snapshots Issues

symptom: ACStor AzureDisk StoragePool stuck with error 'Waiting for pod <diskpool-worker-pod> to get into Running state' when describing the storage pool; diskpool-worker pod not in Running state
rootCause: The diskpool-worker pod managed by capacity-provisioner is not running; capacity-provisioner deployment may have insufficient replicas or be unhealthy
solution: Check diskpool-worker pod: kubectl get pods -l acstor.azure.com/managedby=capacity-provisioner -o wide -n acstor; describe the pod if not running; ensure capacity-provisioner has 2 replicas: kubectl scale deployment -n acstor acstor-capacity-provisioner --replicas=2
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Creating Or Deleting StoragePools Issues

symptom: ACStor EphemeralDisk StoragePool creation fails with 'No block devices found' when describing the storage pool; no disk pool created by 'kubectl get dp -n acstor'
rootCause: The VMs in the node pool used for EphemeralDisk StoragePool do not have NVMe drives; EphemeralDisk requires storage-optimized VM sizes with NVMe
solution: Create a node pool using storage-optimized VMs with NVMe support (see https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/sizes-storage); then retry StoragePool creation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Creating Or Deleting StoragePools Issues

symptom: ACStor ElasticSan StoragePool creation fails; capacity-provisioner logs show 'Maximum possible number of Elastic SAN for the Subscription created already'
rootCause: Hit the maximum number of Elastic SAN deployments allowed per subscription per region
solution: Check Elastic SAN scale targets for the region (https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/storage/elastic-san/elastic-san-scale-targets); either use a different region/subscription or delete unused Elastic SAN instances
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Creating Or Deleting StoragePools Issues

symptom: ACStor Data Integrity Scrubber failure; log messages indicating 'volume replicas or children checksums go out of sync or fail to match'; core-agent logs show 'Replicas of volume <id> are out of sync for segments [...]'
rootCause: ACStor background scrubber detects replica checksums don't match; if out-of-sync spans all replicas (no quorum) auto-rebuild cannot proceed; may be triggered when IOs modify blocks during scrub window
solution: Bring up the application pod and let it run for several hours — scrubber auto-mitigates by partial rebuild from healthy replicas (estimate: ~8min/GB). Monitor core-agent logs for '...completed up to N segment'. If all replicas out of sync (no quorum for rebuild), use volume snapshot to recover the volume
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Data Integrity Scrubber Failure

symptom: ACStor data integrity failure — filesystem becomes read-only or writes fail; io-engine logs show 'WRV-DI: data integrity request ... failed for nexus <id>'
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-acstor-data-integrity-failure.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Data Integrity Failure

symptom: ACStor DiskPool stuck in Creating/Unknown state; storage pool shows READY=False; diskpool shows STATUS=Creating indefinitely
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-acstor-diskpool-create-failure.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Diskpool Create Failure

symptom: ACStor StoragePool/DiskPool deletion fails with 'admission webhook denied: StoragePool is in use and cannot be deleted. Delete all associated PVs first'; or deletion hangs indefinitely
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-acstor-diskpool-delete-failure.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Diskpool Delete

symptom: Azure Container Storage (ACStor) CLI operations fail with error codes such as InvalidArgumentValueError, MutuallyExclusiveArgumentError, RequiredArgumentMissingError, ArgumentUsageError, or UnknownError during enable/disable operations
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-acstor-error-codes.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Error Codes

symptom: ACStor control plane is down; etcd pod count drops below quorum (less than 2 of 3 etcd pods running)
rootCause: ACStor uses etcd as metadata store and requires at least 2 of 3 etcd pods for control plane availability; pod failures reduce quorum below the required threshold
solution: 1) If etcdr pods found but not Running, delete them and wait for auto-recreation (~1-2 min) and cleanup. 2) If no etcdr pods exist, restart etcd-operator pod in acstor namespace and wait for etcdr pod creation and subsequent cleanup. 3) If neither resolves, escalate to engineering (xcontainerstordev@microsoft.com).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Etcd Recovery

symptom: ACStor volume file system becomes read-only after write failures; dmesg shows 'EXT4-fs (nvme1n1): Remounting filesystem read-only'
rootCause: Write failure due to a transient issue in the cluster; if io-engine logs show 'I/O failed with error', may indicate data corruption requiring engineering escalation
solution: 1) Restart the application pod and verify IOs succeed. 2) If issue persists, move the application pod to a different node. 3) If still persists, escalate to engineering. Note: if io-engine logs show data corruption errors, escalate immediately.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/File System Remounted Read Only

symptom: ACStor installation fails with error 'ACStor is not supported in this region'
rootCause: ACStor (public preview) is not available in all Azure regions
solution: Check the regional availability documentation at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/container-storage/container-storage-introduction#regional-availability and install ACStor in one of the supported regions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Installation Failures

symptom: ACStor installation: azuresan-csi-driver pods not Running with authorization error 'does not have authorization to perform action Microsoft.ElasticSan/locations/asyncoperations/'
rootCause: AKS nodepool managed identity not assigned the Contributor Azure RBAC built-in role on the Azure Elastic SAN Preview subscription
solution: Assign Contributor role to the AKS nodepool managed identity on the Azure Elastic SAN Preview subscription. Instructions: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/container-storage/use-container-storage-with-elastic-san#assign-contributor-role-to-aks-managed-identity-on-azure-elastic-san-preview-subscription
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Installation Failures

symptom: ACStor installation via Helm fails with 'client rate limiter Wait returned an error: rate: Wait(n=1) would exceed context deadline' in ExtensionOperationFailed
rootCause: Node pool not properly labeled or VM size does not meet ACStor requirements (minimum 4 vCPUs)
solution: Check node pool labeling instructions at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/container-storage/install-container-storage-aks?tabs=portal#label-the-node-pool and ensure the VM size meets requirements (minimum 4 vCPUs).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Installation Failures

symptom: ACStor installation fails with 'Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration provider has not been registered' or 'InvalidAuthenticationToken' with claim errors
rootCause: Required Azure resource providers (Microsoft.ContainerService, Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration) not registered in the subscription
solution:
Run the following commands to register providers:
`az provider register --namespace Microsoft.ContainerService --wait`
`az provider register --namespace Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration --wait`
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Installation Failures

symptom: ACStor installation: one or more acstor-preq pods not Running with events 'Failed to load kernel modules, the node may need to be rebooted' or 'A reboot is pending for upgrade completion'
rootCause: A Linux upgrade is in progress and the required NVMe kernel modules are not yet available; node reboot is required to complete the upgrade
solution: Wait for the Linux upgrade to complete, reboot the affected node, then retry the ACStor installation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Installation Failures

symptom: ACStor: volume restored from snapshot has mounting failure with error 'requested volume size %s cannot exceed the restore size %s of the source snapshot. Error code: OUT_OF_RANGE'
rootCause: The PVC size for the restored volume must exactly match the original PVC size; requesting a different size (even larger) is not allowed when restoring from snapshot
solution: Redeploy the statefulset using a restored PVC that requests a volume size exactly equal to the original PVC size.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Mounting or Unmounting PersistentVolumes Issues

symptom: ACStor ElasticSan storage pool has throttling or performance issues
rootCause: The storageclass numsessions parameter is configured below the maximum value of 32, limiting I/O throughput
solution: Describe the storageclass of the ElasticSan storagepool and check the value of 'parameters.numsessions' (range 1-32, default 32). Recommend increasing the value to 32 to maximize throughput.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Performance and throttling improvement

symptom: ACStor NVMe replication workload pod stuck in Pending state with error '507 Insufficient Storage: Not enough replicas available, available:2, requested:3'
rootCause: Insufficient node count in the cluster; ACStor creates each replica on a different node, so node count must be >= replica count to provision all replicas
solution: Check 'kubectl get nodes' output. Increase the number of nodes in the cluster to at least match the replica count. Each new node will add a disk pool to the storage pool enabling additional replicas.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Replication

symptom: ACStor replication workload pod stuck in Pending state with 'Not enough replicas available' despite sufficient node count
rootCause: Storage nodes missing the required 'acstor.azure.com/io-engine=acstor' label; ACStor only provisions storage on labeled nodes
solution: Verify with 'kubectl get nodes' that all userpool nodes have the 'acstor.azure.com/io-engine=acstor' label. Add the missing label with: kubectl label nodes <nodename> acstor.azure.com/io-engine=acstor
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Replication

symptom: ACStor issue not documented or mitigation steps fail to resolve the problem
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-acstor-tsg-escalation-steps.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Escalation steps

symptom: ACStor control plane down, etcd pod count below quorum (less than 2), etcdr pods not entering Running state
rootCause: ACStor etcd pod count below quorum; needs at least 2 etcd pods for control plane to run
solution: 1) If etcdr pods exist but not Running: delete them and wait for recreation/cleanup; 2) If no etcdr pods: restart etcd-operator pod to trigger etcdr creation; 3) If both fail: contact xcontainerstordev@microsoft.com
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Etcd Recovery

symptom: ACStor volume filesystem remounted read-only after write failure; dmesg shows: EXT4-fs (nvme1n1): Remounting filesystem read-only
rootCause: Transient write failure (NVMe IO error) triggers kernel read-only remount; io-engine bdev_uring IO failed may indicate data corruption
solution: 1) Restart application pod and verify IO; 2) Move app pod to a different node; 3) If io-engine shows bdev_uring IO failed: escalate via Data Integrity Failure TSG to engineering team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/File System Remounted Read Only

symptom: ACStor installation returns error: ACStor is not supported in this region
rootCause: ACStor is in public preview and not available in all Azure regions
solution: Check ACStor regional availability at learn.microsoft.com and install in a supported region

symptom: azuresan-csi-driver pods not Running; error: does not have authorization to perform action Microsoft.ElasticSan/locations/asyncoperations/ over scope
rootCause: AKS nodepool managed identity missing Contributor role on Azure Elastic SAN Preview subscription
solution: Assign Contributor role to AKS nodepool managed identity on Azure Elastic SAN Preview subscription

symptom: ACStor install ExtensionOperationFailed: Helm installation failed - client rate limiter Wait would exceed context deadline
rootCause: Client rate limiter triggered during Helm installation causing timeout and uninstall
solution: Retry the ACStor installation (transient rate limiting issue)

symptom: ACStor install error: Extensions cannot be used because Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration provider has not been registered, or InvalidAuthenticationToken
rootCause: Required Azure resource providers (Microsoft.ContainerService or Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration) not registered
solution: Run: az provider register --namespace Microsoft.ContainerService --wait AND az provider register --namespace Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration --wait

symptom: acstor-preq pods not Running; describe shows: Failed to load kernel modules OR A reboot is pending for upgrade completion
rootCause: Linux upgrade in progress; NVMe kernel modules not available until node reboot completes the upgrade
solution: Wait for Linux upgrade to complete, reboot the affected node, then retry ACStor installation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Installation Failures

symptom: ACStor PVC restored from snapshot fails to mount: requested volume size cannot exceed the restore size of the source snapshot. Error code: OUT_OF_RANGE
rootCause: PVC restore must request exactly the same capacity as original PVC; cannot exceed snapshot restore size
solution: Redeploy StatefulSet using restored PVC with the same volume size as the original PVC
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Mounting or Unmounting PersistentVolumes Issues

symptom: ACStor ElasticSan storage pool showing throttling or degraded IO performance
rootCause: StorageClass parameters.numsessions configured below maximum (32), limiting concurrent iSCSI sessions
solution: Check parameters.numsessions in ElasticSan storagepool StorageClass (range 1-32, default 32); increase to 32 for maximum iSCSI concurrency
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Performance and throttling improvement

symptom: ACStor NVMe multi-replica workload Pod stuck in Pending; PVC ProvisioningFailed: 507 Insufficient Storage - Not enough replicas available, available:2, requested:3
rootCause: Number of nodes with io-engine label is less than requested replica count; each replica must be placed on a different node
solution: Increase cluster node count to be >= replica count; nodes must have label acstor.azure.com/io-engine=acstor
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Replication

symptom: Sufficient node count but ACStor NVMe replica Pod still Pending; PVC event: Not enough replicas available
rootCause: User pool nodes missing required label acstor.azure.com/io-engine=acstor; capacity provisioner ignores unlabeled nodes
solution: Add storage label to eligible nodes: kubectl label nodes <nodename> acstor.azure.com/io-engine=acstor
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACStor/TSG/Replication

symptom: AKS Fleet Member managed identity changed; Fleet Hub unable to propagate objects to that member cluster
rootCause: Fleet Hub uses original identity to communicate with Member; identity change invalidates authentication causing propagation failure
solution: Remove the affected cluster from Fleet then re-add it to Fleet
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS Fleet Manager/FAQ

symptom: ClusterResourcePlacement (CRP) not propagating; describe CRP shows: the placement did not select any resource or cluster
rootCause: Resources referenced by CRP do not exist in Hub cluster; CRP cannot propagate non-existent resources
solution: Ensure all resources to propagate (Namespace, Deployment etc.) exist in Hub cluster first; CRP will then sync to Member clusters
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS Fleet Manager/FAQ

symptom: AKS Fleet Manager operational questions: CRP propagation, Hub/Member cluster behavior, identity issues, Pod creation restrictions on Hub
solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-aks-fleet-manager-faq.md

symptom: Customer cannot connect to AKS Fleet Manager after retrieving kubeconfig via `az fleet get-credentials`; device code authentication prompt appears, or sees 'sign-in was successful but does not meet criteria to access this resource' error when organization enforces Conditional Access Policies blocking device code flows
rootCause: `az fleet get-credentials` generates a kubeconfig using device code authentication via kubelogin by default. Enterprise Conditional Access Policies (MFA enforcement, blocking device code flows, compliant-device requirements) break this login flow. Fleet Manager relies on ARM-based authentication, so kubeconfigs inherit the older device code mechanism unless explicitly switched.
solution: Run `kubelogin convert-kubeconfig -l azurecli` to update the kubeconfig to use Azure CLI authentication instead of device code authentication. This bypasses the device code prompt and uses the existing Azure CLI login session.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS Fleet Manager/TSG/Cannot Connect FM

symptom: Customer approved a Fleet Manager update run gate but the update run status is still pending/unchanged after approval
rootCause: Gate approval and update run status change happen asynchronously via ServiceBus (GateDoneEvent message). Customer may be querying the update run status immediately after sending the approval, before the async handler has processed the event.
solution: Wait approximately 1 second before re-querying the update run status. If still pending, investigate async errors via Kusto: `FleetAsyncContextActivityEvents | where TIMESTAMP > ago(1d) | where messageType == 'GateDoneEvent' | project-reorder TIMESTAMP, msg`. Async errors are not surfaced to customers but show in this table.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS Fleet Manager/TSG/Debug Gates and Approvals

symptom: AKS Fleet ResourcePlacement fails to place resources on member clusters with namespace not found error
rootCause: ResourcePlacement can only place namespace-scoped resources to clusters that already have the target namespace. If the namespace does not exist on the member cluster, ResourcePlacement cannot proceed. Namespace deployment must be done first via ClusterResourcePlacement.
solution: Use ClusterResourcePlacement (CRP) to deploy namespaces across the fleet first (with `selectionScope: NamespaceOnly`), then create ResourcePlacement objects for resources within those namespaces. Verify namespace exists on member clusters via `kubectl get ns <namespace>`.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS Fleet Manager/TSG/ResourcePlacement

symptom: Creating AKS Fleet auto-upgrade profile in TargetKubernetesVersion channel fails with error `TargetK8sVersionNotSupported` for a version that does not yet exist (e.g., 1.40)
rootCause: Fleet Auto-upgrade TargetKubernetesVersion channel only supports Kubernetes versions in the range [N, N-1, N-2] where N is the latest non-preview release. Requesting an unreleased version is not supported.
solution: Run `az aks get-versions --location <location>` to find the latest non-preview version (N). Choose a target version within [N, N-1, N-2]. Versions with `isPreview: true` do not count as N.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS Fleet Manager/TSG/TargetK8sVersionNotSupported Error

symptom: Creating AKS Fleet auto-upgrade profile in TargetKubernetesVersion channel fails with `TargetK8sVersionNotSupported` for an older version that is past end of official support (e.g., 1.29)
rootCause: Versions earlier than N-2 are considered out of official support by Fleet auto-upgrade. These versions can only be targeted if they support LongTermSupport (LTS) AND `longTermSupport` is enabled in the profile AND all managed clusters have LTS enabled.
solution: (1) Verify version supports LTS: `az aks get-versions --location <location>` — check if `capabilities` contains `AKSLongTermSupport`. (2) Enable `longTermSupport` in the Fleet auto-upgrade profile. (3) Enable LongTermSupport on ALL managed clusters in the fleet. All three conditions are required.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS Fleet Manager/TSG/TargetK8sVersionNotSupported Error

symptom: An existing AKS Fleet auto-upgrade profile that was previously working now fails to generate update runs with `TargetK8sVersionNotSupported` error in the auto-upgrade profile status
rootCause: After the auto-upgrade profile was created, new Kubernetes versions were released, causing the target version to shift from officially supported to LTS-only (beyond N-2). The profile does not have `longTermSupport` enabled, so it can no longer generate update runs for this version.
solution: Update the Fleet auto-upgrade profile to enable `longTermSupport`. Also enable LongTermSupport on all managed clusters in the fleet. Without both, update run generation will continue to fail.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS Fleet Manager/TSG/TargetK8sVersionNotSupported Error

symptom: Customer is unable to remove an AKS cluster from an AKS Fleet; `az fleet member delete` appears to be accepted at ARM level but the fleet member remains in Failed state and is never removed
rootCause: A Delete Lock exists on the target AKS cluster resource. Although `az fleet member delete` only removes the cluster from the fleet (does not delete the cluster), the operation uses HTTP DELETE method at the ARM level, which triggers resource lock protection and is blocked by Delete Locks on the target resource.
solution: (1) Remove the Delete Lock from the AKS cluster resource to be removed from the fleet. (2) Re-run `az fleet member delete`. (3) If desired, re-apply the lock after successful removal. Diagnosis via Kusto: `cluster('armprodgbl.eastus').database('ARMProd').Unionizer('Requests','EventServiceEntries')` to check ARM requests, then `FleetAsyncQoSEvents` to see the lock error.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS Fleet Manager/TSG/Unable to Remove AKS Cluster from Fleet
