symptom: AKS tunnel disconnected: kubectl logs/exec/port-forward/cp/top fail with dial tcp nodeIP:10250: i/o timeout. Webhook admission controllers stop working. HPA not functioning due to metrics-server unavailability.
rootCause: AKS tunnel (V1/OpenVPN/Konnectivity) between worker nodes and control plane is disconnected. Common causes: egress rules blocking required tunnel ports, worker node hosting tunnel pod is resource-exhausted, or tunnel pod crash-looping.
solution: 1) Identify tunnel type: kubectl get po -n kube-system (tunnelfront=V1, aks-link=OpenVPN, konnectivity-agent=Konnectivity). 2) Check tunnel pod logs. 3) Validate egress: TCP 9000 (V1), UDP 1194 (OpenVPN), TCP 443 (Konnectivity). 4) Verify node resources. 5) If overloaded, cordon/drain to reschedule tunnel pod.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/Tunnel TSG for AKS secure networking

symptom: AKS Tunnel V1 (tunnelfront) SSH connection fails: logs show ssh: connect to host ... port 9000: Connection timed out.
rootCause: TCP port 9000 egress from worker node to AKS control plane is blocked by NSG, firewall, or network appliance.
solution: 1) Verify TCP 9000 outbound allowed in NSG/firewall. 2) Check tunnelfront pod logs. 3) From node, test port 9000 reachability to API server FQDN. 4) Review AKS egress requirements.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/Tunnel TSG for AKS secure networking

symptom: AKS OpenVPN tunnel (aks-link) fails: TLS Error: TLS key negotiation failed to occur within 60 seconds.
rootCause: UDP port 1194 egress blocked or unreliable. OpenVPN uses UDP 1194. Network instability or packet loss causes TLS negotiation timeout.
solution: 1) Verify UDP 1194 outbound allowed. 2) Check aks-link pod logs. 3) Test UDP 1194 connectivity. 4) If using Azure Firewall/NVA, ensure UDP 1194 not dropped. 5) Consider upgrading to Konnectivity (TCP 443).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/Tunnel TSG for AKS secure networking

symptom: Customer using BYOCNI on AKS encounters CNI-related networking issues. Microsoft support cannot assist.
rootCause: AKS with BYOCNI uses third-party CNI plugin. Microsoft has no visibility/control over third-party CNI.
solution: BYOCNI has explicit support implications: MS support cannot assist with CNI issues. Engage CNI vendor. Recommend migrating to Kubenet or Azure CNI for full support.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] Aks and Network team common troubleshooting/2 - What is the Network deployment type used in the AKS cluster

symptom: Persistent connectivity issues in AKS with Azure Firewall application rules. Nodes reuse source ports causing connections to fail intermittently.
rootCause: AKS nodes reuse ephemeral ports. Azure Firewall application rules cause SNAT port reuse conflicts, dropping/stalling connections.
solution: 1) Review AZFW+AKS common issues TSG. 2) Use network rules instead of application rules. 3) Gather AZFW packet captures. 4) Engage Networking SE.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] Aks and Network team common troubleshooting/7 - Troubleshoot Azure Firewall

symptom: DNS resolution fails in AKS: NXDOMAIN error. Pod cannot reach services by hostname.
rootCause: DNS record missing, zone misconfigured, CoreDNS forwarding misconfigured, or Private DNS Zone not linked to VNet.
solution: 1) nslookup/dig from pod and node. 2) Check CoreDNS logs/configmap. 3) Verify Private DNS Zone linked to VNet. 4) Jarvis Azure DNS stats. 5) See Random DNS Query Failures TSG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] Azure DNS

symptom: DNS resolution fails in AKS: connection timed out; no servers could be reached.
rootCause: Azure DNS resolver (168.63.129.16) unreachable. NSG blocking UDP 53, custom DNS server down, CoreDNS unhealthy, or node networking issue.
solution: 1) Check CoreDNS pods running. 2) Test DNS from node vs pod to isolate. 3) NSG allows UDP/TCP 53. 4) Verify custom DNS reachable. 5) Jarvis Azure DNS stats.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] Azure DNS

symptom: AKS 集群升级到 Kubernetes 1.27+ 后，使用 Azure NPM (network-policy=azure) 的 External LoadBalancer/NodePort Service (externalTrafficPolicy: Cluster) 出现 TCP 重传、Health Probe 间歇性失败、外部流量被丢弃
rootCause: Kubernetes 1.27+ 中 Azure NPM 将 iptables AZURE-NPM chain 的跳转位置调整到 kube-services 之前 (PlaceAzureChainFirst=true)，导致 NetworkPolicy 在 kube-services DNAT 之前就对入站流量进行评估；如果 NetworkPolicy 的 Ingress 规则没有显式允许 LB Health Probe IP (168.63.129.16) 和外部客户端 IP，流量会被 NPM 直接丢弃
solution: 在隔离 LB Service 后端 Pod 的 NetworkPolicy 中添加 Ingress allow 规则：1) 添加 Azure LB Health Probe IP 168.63.129.16/32；2) 添加客户端 IP CIDR 范围；3) 如果流量经过 SNAT 到节点 IP，还需添加节点 IP CIDR。如果客户端 IP 未知，可添加端口级别的 allow 规则 (ingress ports protocol+port) 作为替代方案。仅影响 externalTrafficPolicy: Cluster，Local 模式不受影响
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] Azure Network Policy Manager/NPM (Network Policy Manager) PlaceAzureChainFirst=True change (1.27+) blocks external access to LoadBalancer services

symptom: AKS 节点上 Pod 创建失败卡在 ContainerCreating 或删除卡在 Terminating，syslog 报错 'netplugin failed with no error message: signal: killed'，Azure CNI 网络插件异常终止
rootCause: 节点上安装了 Palo Alto 安全软件（Traps 或 Cortex Agent），该软件将 Azure CNI 二进制文件 /opt/cni/bin/azure-vnet 识别为不受信任的程序并终止其进程，导致 Pod sandbox 网络配置/销毁操作失败
solution: 1) 联系 Palo Alto 支持将 /opt/cni/bin/azure-vnet 二进制文件添加到 Palo Alto Traps/Cortex Agent 的允许列表 (exception list) 中；2) 永久解决方案是从 AKS 节点上移除 Palo Alto 安全软件。诊断方法：在 IaaS Disk Inspect (AKS + Diagnostics 模式) 中检查 azure-vnet.log 和 syslog，确认 CNI binary 被终止的时间戳与 sandbox 操作失败时间吻合
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] netplugin failed with no error message: signal: killed

symptom: AKS 升级到 Kubernetes 1.27+ 后，使用 Azure NPM (network-policy=azure) 的集群中，External LoadBalancer/NodePort Service (externalTrafficPolicy: Cluster) 的 Health Probe 成功率下降，外部流量被丢弃（TCP Retransmissions）
rootCause: NPM v1.4.32+ 将 AZURE-NPM iptables chain 前置于 kube-services chain (PlaceAzureChainFirst=true)。如果 NetworkPolicy 的 Ingress 规则未放行 LB Health Probe IP (168.63.129.16) 和外部客户端 IP，流量在到达 kube-services 之前就被 NPM 规则 DROP。此前 kube-services 先于 NPM 处理，外部入站流量不受 NetworkPolicy 限制。
solution: 在目标 Pod 的 NetworkPolicy 中添加 Ingress allow 规则：1) 放行 Azure LB Health Probe IP 168.63.129.16/32；2) 放行客户端源 IP/CIDR；3) 如果流量被 SNAT 到 Node IP，放行 Node IP CIDR。或使用 port-level allow rule: ingress.ports[].port=<SERVICE TARGET PORT>。externalTrafficPolicy: Local 的 Service 不受影响。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] Azure Network Policy Manager/NPM (Network Policy Manager) PlaceAzureChainFirst=True change (1.27+) blocks external access to LoadBalancer services

symptom: AKS 集群中跨节点 Pod 通信时，目标 Pod 看到的源 IP 是节点 IP 而非 Pod IP（意外 SNAT/MASQUERADE）
rootCause: ip-masq-agent 的 ConfigMap (azure-ip-masq-agent-config) 中 nonMasqueradeCIDRs 未包含 Pod CIDR，导致 Pod-to-Pod 跨节点流量被 iptables IP-MASQ-AGENT chain MASQUERADE。
solution: 1) 在节点上运行 iptables-save | grep IP-MASQ-AGENT 检查 RETURN 规则中的 CIDR 列表；2) kubectl get cm azure-ip-masq-agent-config -n kube-system -o yaml 检查 nonMasqueradeCIDRs 是否包含 Node CIDR、Pod CIDR 和 Service CIDR；3) 如果不匹配，修正 ConfigMap 或 escalate。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] IP-masq-agent

symptom: AKS Pod 没有出站连接（outbound connectivity failure），且集群使用 ip-masq-agent
rootCause: ip-masq-agent 的 iptables 规则中 nonMasqueradeCIDRs 配置错误，导致出站流量的 MASQUERADE 规则异常（目标 IP 匹配了错误的 RETURN 规则，或 MASQUERADE 未正确执行）。
solution: 1) iptables-save 检查 IP-MASQ-AGENT chain 是否存在及规则是否正确；2) 验证 ConfigMap 的 nonMasqueradeCIDRs 与集群实际 CIDR 一致；3) 检查 CCP Log (ControlPlaneEventsNonShoebox/Shoebox) 是否有相关配置变更记录。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] IP-masq-agent

symptom: AKS 集群因 NSG 安全规则配置不当导致集群管理流量被阻断（LB 访问、控制平面通信、出站流量异常）
rootCause: 客户在 NIC 或 Subnet 级别的 NSG 中添加了自定义拒绝规则，阻断了 AKS 所需的出站 FQDN/端口或入站管理流量。
solution: 1) 使用 ASC Resource Explorer 找到 nodepool 关联的 NSG（NIC 级别和 Subnet 级别）；2) 验证安全规则允许 AKS 所需的出站流量（参考 docs: limit-egress-traffic#required-outbound-network-rules-and-fqdns-for-aks-clusters）；3) 检查可选 addon/feature 的额外规则需求；4) 默认规则通常没问题，重点检查自定义规则。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] Network Security Group (NSG)

symptom: AKS Private Cluster 无法连接 API Server：DNS lookup failure（无法解析 API Server FQDN）
rootCause: Private DNS Zone 未与集群 VNet 关联，或 DNS Zone 中缺少/错误的 A 记录指向 Private Endpoint IP。
solution: 1) az network private-dns link vnet list 检查 DNS Zone 是否链接到正确的 VNet (virtualNetworkLinkState=Completed)；2) az network private-dns record-set a list 检查 A 记录是否存在且 IP 正确；3) 确认 metadata 中 own-aks-cluster 指向正确的集群。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] PrivateLink Cluster

symptom: AKS Private Cluster 无法连接 API Server：连接超时（DNS 解析成功但 TCP 443 不通）
rootCause: Private Endpoint 或其 NIC 配置异常（subnet 不匹配、privateLinkServiceConnection 状态非 Approved），或网络层问题阻断了到 Private Endpoint IP 的连接。
solution: 1) az network private-endpoint list 检查 PE 的 provisioningState=Succeeded、privateLinkServiceConnectionState.status=Approved；2) az network nic list 验证 PE NIC 的 subnet 与集群 subnet 一致；3) 从同 subnet 的 VM/Node 运行 nc -vz <apiserver-fqdn> 443 测试连通性；4) 如以上正常，engage networking team 排查网络层问题。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] PrivateLink Cluster

symptom: AKS 节点 Pod 创建卡在 ContainerCreating 或删除卡在 Terminating，syslog 报 'netplugin failed with no error message: signal: killed'（Azure CNI 插件）
rootCause: Palo Alto 安全软件（Traps 或 Cortex Agent）杀死了 Azure CNI 二进制文件 /opt/cni/bin/azure-vnet，导致 IP 分配失败、sandbox 操作失败。Traps 会在 syslog 中留下 kill 记录；Cortex Agent 可能无 syslog 记录，需通过 azure-vnet.log 时间戳关联。
solution: 1) 检查 syslog 和 azure-vnet.log 确认 CNI binary 被意外终止的时间段；2) 确认节点安装了 Palo Alto Traps 或 Cortex Agent；3) 请客户联系 Palo Alto 将 /opt/cni/bin/azure-vnet 添加到允许列表；4) 永久解决方案是从 AKS 节点移除 Palo Alto 软件。注：需 Inspect IaaS Disk (AKS+Diagnostics mode) 获取 azure-vnet.log 和完整 syslog。
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] netplugin failed with no error message: signal: killed

symptom: AKS virtual node 创建失败: error creating provider: error setting up network: unable to delegate subnet to Azure Container Instance since it references the route table
rootCause: ACI (virtual node) 子网不支持 User-Defined Routing (UDR)。aci-connector 会校验并阻止使用关联了路由表的子网进行委派
solution: 从 ACI virtual node 使用的子网中移除 User-Defined Routing (路由表)。ACI 服务不支持子网上的 UDR

symptom: AGIC 场景下通过 Application Gateway 访问后端应用返回 HTTP 404 Not Found，请求路径与后端应用期望路径不匹配
rootCause: Application Gateway 将请求转发到后端时使用了客户端原始路径，但后端应用期望在不同的路径上接收请求
solution: 在 ingress resource 上添加 appgw.ingress.kubernetes.io/backend-path-prefix 注解，将外部请求路径重写为后端应用实际期望的路径。也可修改应用配置使其在请求的路径上监听，或修改客户端请求 URL
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 404 Not Found

symptom: AGIC 场景下通过 Application Gateway 访问后端应用返回 HTTP 502 Bad Gateway，特别是在 K8s 工作负载滚动更新期间频繁出现
rootCause: Application Gateway 后端池中没有健康的后端成员。在 K8s rolling update 期间旧 Pod 已终止但新 Pod 尚未就绪时，会短暂出现无可用后端的情况
solution: 1. 检查 AppGW Backend Health 确认后端健康状态。2. 如果 502 在 rolling update 期间频发，按照 AGIC 'Minimizing Downtime During Deployments' 文档配置 preStop hook、readinessProbe 和 minReadySeconds 来减少部署期间的停机时间
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 502 Bad Gateway

symptom: AGIC 场景下通过 Application Gateway 访问后端应用返回 HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout，响应时间与配置的 request-timeout 一致
rootCause: Application Gateway 的 request timeout（默认 30s）过短，后端应用处理请求耗时超过该超时值
solution: 在 ingress resource 上设置 appgw.ingress.kubernetes.io/request-timeout 注解增大超时值（单位秒）。通过 kubectl describe ingress 确认当前值，在 Azure Portal AppGW Backend Settings 中验证生效。如果 504 时间与 timeout 不一致，需排查后端应用本身问题
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 504 Gateway Timeout

symptom: AGIC pod 日志显示 ErrorGetApplicationGatewayError: Failed fetching configuration for Application Gateway，伴随 dial timeout 错误和 Retrying in 10s
rootCause: AGIC pod 无法通过 TCP 443 连接到 Azure Resource Manager (management.azure.com)。通常由 AKS 集群出站流量限制导致：UDR 指向 NVA/Azure Firewall 但未放行 ARM 端点、NSG 阻止了 AzureResourceManager service tag、或 Network Policy 阻止了 AGIC pod 的出站流量
solution: 1. 如 outbound type 为 UDR，检查 NVA/Firewall 规则是否允许到 management.azure.com:443 的流量。2. 如 outbound type 为 LoadBalancer，检查子网路由表是否有 0.0.0.0/0 → NVA 的路由。3. 检查 NSG 出站规则是否阻止了 AzureResourceManager service tag。4. 如启用了 Network Policy，检查是否有策略阻止 AGIC pod 出站到 ARM IP 范围
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod 日志显示 AuthorizationFailed 错误，AGIC 无法获取或更新 Application Gateway 配置
rootCause: AGIC 使用的 Managed Identity 缺少必要的 RBAC 角色分配。需要 Contributor 角色在 Application Gateway 资源上，Reader 角色在 Application Gateway 所在资源组上
solution: 为 AGIC identity 添加角色分配：1. 在 Application Gateway 上分配 Contributor 角色。2. 在 Application Gateway 所在 Resource Group 上分配 Reader 角色。对于 AGIC addon 启用的集群，managed identity 名称格式为 ingressapplicationgateway-<CLUSTER_NAME>
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AKS virtual node fails with 'error creating provider: error setting up network: unable to delegate subnet to Azure Container Instance since it references the route table' - aci-connector-linux pod fatal error
rootCause: ACI does not support user-defined routing (UDR). The aci-connector has a validation check that blocks subnet delegation to ACI when a route table is associated with the virtual node subnet.
solution: Remove the user-defined routing (route table association) from the subnet used for ACI virtual node. UDR is not supported on the ACI subnet for virtual nodes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/error setting up network - AKS virtual node

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'ErrorGetApplicationGatewayError' with 'Failed fetching configuration for Application Gateway' followed by 'dial timeout' and 'Retrying in 10s' - AGIC cannot configure Application Gateway
rootCause: Network connectivity from AGIC pod to Azure Resource Manager endpoint (management.azure.com:443) is blocked. Common causes: UDR routing through NVA/Azure Firewall without allowing ARM traffic, NSG denying AzureResourceManager service tag, or Network Policies blocking AGIC pod outbound.
solution: Check and fix egress rules: (1) If UDR outbound, verify NVA/Firewall allows traffic to management.azure.com; (2) If LB outbound with route table 0.0.0.0/0 to NVA, check NVA rules; (3) Verify NSG allows AzureResourceManager service tag; (4) Check Network Policies do not block AGIC pod outbound on port 443.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'AuthorizationFailed' error when trying to fetch or apply configuration to Application Gateway
rootCause: Missing required RBAC role assignments for AGIC identity. AGIC needs: Contributor on Application Gateway scope, Reader on Application Gateway Resource Group.
solution: Create the missing role assignments: assign AGIC managed identity as Contributor on the Application Gateway resource and Reader on the Application Gateway resource group. For AGIC addon clusters, the identity name is 'ingressapplicationgateway-<CLUSTER_NAME>'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'invalid_client' error - Application Gateway configuration updates fail due to authentication issue
rootCause: The Service Principal secret used by AGIC to authenticate with Azure has expired.
solution: Generate a new secret for the Service Principal and update AGIC to use the new secret following: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/ingress-controller-install-existing#using-a-service-principal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'Ignore mutating App Gateway as it is not mutable' and 'Application Gateway is in stopped state' - AGIC cannot apply configuration changes
rootCause: Application Gateway is in Stopped operational state (customer may have stopped it via CLI to reduce costs). AGIC requires AppGW to be in Running state.
solution: Start the Application Gateway: `az network application-gateway start -g <RG> -n <NAME>`. Verify AGIC pod logs show successful deployment after AppGW returns to Running state.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AKS virtual node fails: error creating provider: error setting up network: unable to delegate subnet to Azure Container Instance since it references the route table. aci-connector-linux pod logs show level=fatal.
rootCause: ACI does not support user-defined routing (UDR). Subnet for ACI virtual nodes has a route table attached, blocking subnet delegation.
solution: Remove the route table association from the subnet used for ACI virtual node. ACI virtual node subnet must not reference any route table.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/error setting up network - AKS virtual node

symptom: AGIC pod logs: ErrorGetApplicationGatewayError / Failed fetching configuration for Application Gateway / dial timeout / Retrying in 10s.
rootCause: AGIC pod lacks TCP/443 connectivity to management.azure.com. Blocked by UDR to NVA/Firewall, NSG denying AzureResourceManager service tag, or K8s Network Policies.
solution: Check: 1) NVA/Firewall rules for management.azure.com:443. 2) Route table 0.0.0.0/0 entries. 3) NSG AzureResourceManager service tag. 4) K8s NetworkPolicies for AGIC pod egress.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod logs show AuthorizationFailed error when configuring Application Gateway.
rootCause: AGIC identity missing role assignments. Required: Contributor on AppGW + Reader on AppGW RG. Addon identity: ingressapplicationgateway-<CLUSTER_NAME>.
solution: Assign Contributor role on AppGW resource and Reader role on AppGW resource group to AGIC identity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod logs show invalid_client error. Application Gateway config updates fail.
rootCause: AGIC uses Service Principal for auth and the SP secret/credential has expired.
solution: Renew SP secret and update AGIC config per MS docs ingress-controller-install-existing#using-a-service-principal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: AGIC pod logs: Ignore mutating App Gateway as it is not mutable / Application Gateway is in stopped state.
rootCause: Application Gateway is in Stopped state. AGIC requires Running state to apply config.
solution: Start AppGW: az network application-gateway start -g <RG> -n <NAME>. Verify: az network application-gateway show --query operationalState.

symptom: AGIC/AppGW returns HTTP 404 Not Found when the request path does not match the backend application expected path
rootCause: The URL path in the client request does not match the path on which the backend application is listening. In a reverse proxy setup (AKS/AGIC/AppGW), the request reaches the correct backend but the app cannot find the resource at that path.
solution: Add 'appgw.ingress.kubernetes.io/backend-path-prefix' annotation to the ingress resource to rewrite the request path. Alternatively, modify the request URL or reconfigure the app to listen on the expected path.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 404 Not Found

symptom: AGIC/AppGW returns HTTP 502 Bad Gateway during Kubernetes rolling deployments
rootCause: During rolling updates, old pods are terminated before AppGW health probes detect the change, leaving no healthy backend pool members temporarily.
solution: Implement 'Minimizing Downtime During Deployments' recommendations from Azure AGIC docs (https://azure.github.io/application-gateway-kubernetes-ingress/how-tos/minimize-downtime-during-deployments/). Check backend health in Azure Portal (Monitoring > Backend health) or ASC.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 502 Bad Gateway

symptom: AGIC/AppGW returns HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout when backend response exceeds configured timeout
rootCause: AppGW enforces a request timeout (default 30s via AGIC annotation). If the backend takes longer to respond, AppGW returns HTTP 504.
solution: Check 'appgw.ingress.kubernetes.io/request-timeout' annotation on the ingress (kubectl describe ingress). Increase timeout if backend legitimately needs more time. Verify in Azure Portal under AppGW > Backend Settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting 504 Gateway Timeout

symptom: AGIC pod logs show 'AuthorizationFailed' error when trying to configure Application Gateway
rootCause: AGIC identity missing required role assignments: Contributor on AppGW resource and Reader on AppGW resource group.
solution: Create role assignments: (1) Contributor to AGIC identity on AppGW. (2) Reader to AGIC identity on AppGW RG. For addon clusters, identity name is 'ingressapplicationgateway-<CLUSTER_NAME>'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/AKS Network Troubleshooting Methodology/[TSG] AGIC/[TSG] AGIC Troubleshooting AGIC AppGw Integration Issues

symptom: Two or more pods mounted to a single PV-PVC using Blob storage (blobfuse-premium) experience sync issues: files modified by one pod are not visible in another pod until restart.
rootCause: FUSE kernel cache prevents sync between pods sharing same blob PV. blobfuse-mounted file system uses kernel cache, changes written by one pod are cached locally and not propagated.
solution: Option 1: Add "-o direct_io" to PV mountOptions to bypass kernel cache (increases API calls). Option 2 (recommended): Upgrade node image to 202507.21.0+ (blobfuse2 >=2.5.0), use mountOptions: --file-cache-timeout-in-seconds=120 --attr-cache-timeout=100 --disable-kernel-cache.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Blob sync issue between pods

symptom: Cluster create or start fails with AKSCapacityError or AKSCapacityHeavyUsage: creating/starting unavailable in region.
rootCause: Target region capacity constrained - running out of compute underlay capacity. Constrained Regions Plan throttles creates to protect existing clusters.
solution: Primary: use another region. Alternatives: retry later (end of day), try different cluster type (public vs private), use EA subscription. QatarCentral extremely constrained until 2026 - create ICM for whitelisting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Create/AKSCapacityError or AKSCapacityHeavyUsage Cluster create blocked in constrained region

symptom: Nodepool creation/scaling fails: ConfigurationVersion not supported for AgentBaker. Blocks new nodepool creation, scaling, reconciliation even with latest NodeImageVersion.
rootCause: AgentBaker internal ConfigurationVersion stored in agent pool profile has been retired. Not a VM image issue. Affects long-lived clusters or those without recent control plane upgrades.
solution: Preferred: upgrade AKS control plane to regenerate agent pool profiles. Alternative: az aks nodepool update on a healthy pool to reconcile. If all pools affected, create ICM to EEE/AKS RP. GitHub: https://github.com/Azure/AgentBaker/issues/6229
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Create/AgentBaker NodePool ConfigVersionError

symptom: Creating nodepool with VM size Standard_E112ibds_v5 fails: DiskControllerTypeMismatchBetweenOsAndVMScaleSetSize.
rootCause: Standard_E112ibds_v5 only supports NVMe disk controller, but AKS node image from PIR defaults to SCSI. SCSI incompatible with NVMe-only sizes.
solution: Use Standard_E112iads_v5 instead (supports both SCSI and NVMe). Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/ebdsv5-ebsv5-series
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Create/DiskControllerTypeMismatchBetweenOsAndVMScaleSetSize_with_size_Standard_E112ibds_v5

symptom: Files/directories in blob storage not syncing between pods sharing the same PV-PVC using BlobFuse. Changes made from one pod not visible in another pod until pod restart.
rootCause: BlobFuse uses kernel cache (FUSE file cache) on the node. When multiple pods mount the same blob PV, each pod uses its own kernel cache, so changes from one pod are not reflected in another pod's cache.
solution: Option 1: Add '-o direct_io' to PV mountOptions to bypass kernel cache (increases API calls to storage account). Option 2: Ensure blobfuse2 >= 2.5.0 (node image >= 202507.21.0) and use '--disable-kernel-cache' with '--file-cache-timeout-in-seconds=120' and '--attr-cache-timeout=100' to sync blob cache periodically across pods.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Blob sync issue between pods

symptom: AKS cluster creation or start operation blocked with error 'AKSCapacityError' or 'AKSCapacityHeavyUsage' in capacity-constrained region.
rootCause: Region is temporarily running out of AKS underlay capacity. AKS has a Constrained Regions Plan that blocks new cluster creates to protect existing clusters from runtime errors.
solution: Primary: Create cluster in a different region (https://aka.ms/aks/regions). Alternatives: (1) Retry later when capacity frees up (e.g. end of day). (2) Try different cluster type (public vs private may use different underlay). (3) Use Enterprise Agreement subscription (EA subs have priority over Free/Azure Plan). For QatarCentral: request ICM whitelist to Annie Bubinski with subscription IDs and cluster count.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Create/AKSCapacityError or AKSCapacityHeavyUsage Cluster create blocked in constrained region

symptom: AKS nodepool creation or scaling fails with error 'ConfigurationVersion v0.xxxxxxxx.x is not supported for AgentBaker. Please upgrade agent pool to the latest NodeImageVersion.' Issue persists even with latest NodeImageVersion specified.
rootCause: Internal AgentBaker configuration version stored with the agent pool profile has been retired from service. AgentBaker validates configurationVersion before evaluating the node image, so this is not a VM image issue.
solution: Primary: Upgrade AKS control plane Kubernetes version (forces regeneration of agent pool profiles with supported configurationVersion). Alternative: If at least one healthy nodepool exists, run 'az aks nodepool update --resource-group <rg> --cluster-name <cluster> --name <existing-pool>' to reconcile stale metadata. If all mitigations fail, create ICM to EEE/AKS RP for service-side reconciliation. Ref: https://github.com/Azure/AgentBaker/issues/6229
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Create/AgentBaker NodePool ConfigVersionError

symptom: Creating AKS nodepool with VM size Standard_E112ibds_v5 fails with error 'InvalidParameter' - 'DiskControllerTypeMismatchBetweenOsAndVMScaleSetSize'. The VMSS cannot boot with OS image.
rootCause: VM size Standard_E112ibds_v5 exclusively supports NVMe disk controller type, while the AKS node OS image does not specify diskcontrollerType, defaulting to SCSI. CRP rejects the VMSS creation due to the mismatch.
solution: Use VM size Standard_E112iads_v5 instead (supports both SCSI and NVMe, less restrictive). Verify VM size disk controller capabilities: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/ebdsv5-ebsv5-series#ebdsv5-series. Query SKU API: https://aka.ms/azure-compute-skus.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Create/DiskControllerTypeMismatchBetweenOsAndVMScaleSetSize_with_size_Standard_E112ibds_v5

symptom: AKS node provisioning fails with VMExtensionProvisioningError, CSE exit status=50, stderr shows 'nc: getaddrinfo: Name or service not known' for multiple required endpoints.
rootCause: Node cannot establish outbound connectivity to required AKS endpoints during bootstrapping. Typically caused by NSG rules blocking outbound, UDR routing through NVA/Firewall without proper allowlists, or DNS misconfiguration.
solution: Verify outbound connectivity requirements (https://aka.ms/adv-network-prerequest): (1) Check NSG for blocks on required AKS FQDN/IP ranges. (2) Verify UDR/Firewall allows traffic to required endpoints. (3) Ensure DNS resolution works for required FQDNs. (4) Disable cluster autoscaler temporarily, manually scale up by 1 instance to isolate the issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/CSE Exit Codes/vmssCSE failures during node provisioning

symptom: AKS node provisioning fails with VMAgentStatusCommunicationError: 'VM has not reported status for VM agent or extensions'. All AKS extensions (AKSLinuxBilling, AKSLinuxExtension, vmssCSE) fail simultaneously on the same VM instance.
rootCause: When all VM extensions fail together on a single instance, it indicates a problem with the VM itself (not extension or connectivity). The VM agent cannot communicate status back to Azure, and the VM is stuck in a failed state.
solution: Identify failed VM instances from error payload (target field shows instance number). Reimage the failed VMs, then retry the operation. Use CRP Kusto query (VmssVMGoalSeekingActivity, callerName=='PollForVMExtensionsProvisioningResult') to identify all affected instances. Customer may also add custom unsupported VM extensions which can block AKS extensions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/CSE Exit Codes/vmssCSE failures during node provisioning

symptom: AKS cluster creation fails with vmssCSE exit status=1. Cloud-init log shows 'Failed to create user' with 'useradd: invalid user name' error for admin username exceeding 32 characters.
rootCause: Linux admin username specified during cluster creation exceeds 32 characters. The Linux useradd command enforces a 32-character limit. AKS frontend validation may not catch this if cluster was created before validation was added or via direct REST API.
solution: Recreate the cluster with an admin username shorter than 32 characters: 'az aks create -g <RG> -n <cluster> --admin-username <username_under_32_chars>'. For existing clusters, this requires cluster recreation. Verify username with Kusto query on FrontEndContextActivity checking linuxProfile.adminUsername.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/CSE Exit Codes/vmssCSE failures during node provisioning

symptom: Two or more pods mounted to a single PV-PVC using Blob storage (blob-fuse-premium StorageClass) do not see each other's file changes; modifications made from Pod 1 are not reflected in Pod 2 until Pod 2 is restarted
rootCause: FUSE kernel cache on the node prevents file changes from being propagated between pods sharing the same Blob PV. The default mountOptions include kernel-level attribute and entry caching (attr_timeout=120, entry_timeout=120) which causes stale reads.
solution: Option 1: Add '-o direct_io' to mountOptions to bypass kernel cache (increases storage API calls). Option 2 (recommended): Upgrade node image to 202507.21.0+ (blobfuse2 2.5.0+), then use mountOptions: --file-cache-timeout-in-seconds=120, --attr-cache-timeout=100, --disable-kernel-cache. This clears blob cache every 120s and syncs across all pods.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/Blob sync issue between pods

symptom: AKS node provisioning fails with vmssCSE exit code 50 (ERR_OUTBOUND_CONN_FAIL). CSE cannot establish outbound connectivity to mcr.microsoft.com:443.
rootCause: Nodes cannot reach mcr.microsoft.com:443 due to NSG blocking outbound, route table forcing traffic to firewall/NVA that blocks it, VNET address range overlapping with public IPs (e.g. 13.0.0.0/8), DNS resolution failure, AAAA/IPv6 blocked on firewall, or VNET encryption enabled with firewall/NVA present.
solution: Run 'curl -v --connect-timeout 10 --insecure --proxy-insecure https://mcr.microsoft.com/v2/' from node. Check NSG/UDR/firewall allows mcr.microsoft.com. If curl works but nodes still fail, check /var/log/azure/cluster-provision-cse-output.log - if no 'Trying $IP:443' line appears, it's a DNS issue: verify with 'nslookup mcr.microsoft.com $DNS_IP'. Disable VNET encryption if firewall/NVA is present.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/CSE Exit Codes/vmssCSE failures during node provisioning

symptom: AKS node provisioning fails with vmssCSE exit code 51 (ERR_K8S_API_SERVER_CONN_FAIL). New nodes cannot connect to API server FQDN on port 443.
rootCause: Node cannot reach the AKS API server due to firewall/NVA blocking, missing outbound IP in authorized IP ranges, or complex network topology (e.g., VNET-to-VNET IPSec tunnel with Palo Alto firewall). If 'Name or service not known' appears, cluster may be in Stopped state.
solution: Validate with 'nc -vz <ClusterFqdn> 443'. If using authorized IP ranges, add the outbound IP of firewall/NVA. If cluster is stopped, execute another Start operation (or Stop, wait 30min, Start). For private clusters, ensure Hub-and-Spoke architecture is correctly configured.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/CSE Exit Codes/vmssCSE failures during node provisioning

symptom: AKS node provisioning fails with vmssCSE exit code 52 (ERR_K8S_API_SERVER_DNS_LOOKUP_FAIL). DNS lookup for API server FQDN fails.
rootCause: Custom DNS server not correctly configured for private AKS cluster. For private clusters with custom DNS, initial cluster creation failure is expected because DNS zone has not been linked to VNET yet. Conditional forwarding may not support subdomains for private link hostname.
solution: Run 'nslookup <ClusterFqdn>' from a node. For private cluster with custom DNS: initial failure is expected, a later PUT operation brings cluster to Succeeded state. Follow docs for Hub-and-Spoke with custom DNS. If conditional forwarding issue, create identical record without subdomain in AKS-managed DNS zone.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/CSE Exit Codes/vmssCSE failures during node provisioning

symptom: AKS node provisioning fails with vmssCSE exit code 25 (ERR_MOBY_APT_LIST_TIMEOUT) with error 'SSL certificate problem: unable to get local issuer certificate' when accessing packages.microsoft.com.
rootCause: SSL inspection tool (e.g., ZScaler proxy) replaces the SSL certificate during HTTPS communication, acting as man-in-the-middle. The modified certificate is not trusted by the node CA store.
solution: Bypass SSL inspection (e.g., ZScaler) for required outbound FQDNs including packages.microsoft.com. Verify with 'curl -v https://packages.microsoft.com/config/ubuntu/18.04/prod.list' and check the certificate issuer.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/CSE Exit Codes/vmssCSE failures during node provisioning

symptom: AKS node provisioning fails with vmssCSE exit code 31 (ERR_K8S_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT). Cannot download Kubernetes binaries from acs-mirror.azureedge.net. Log shows 'Cannot open: No such file or directory' for tar file.
rootCause: NVA or firewall blocking access to acs-mirror.azureedge.net which is required for downloading Kubernetes binaries during node provisioning.
solution: Have customer verify connectivity: 'curl -v --connect-timeout 10 https://acs-mirror.azureedge.net/kubernetes/v1.xx.xx/binaries/kubernetes-node-linux-amd64.tar.gz'. Ensure firewall/NVA allows access to acs-mirror.azureedge.net per Azure Global required FQDN rules.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/CSE Exit Codes/vmssCSE failures during node provisioning

symptom: AKS cluster create or start fails with error AKSCapacityError or AKSCapacityHeavyUsage: 'Creating a new cluster or start cluster is unavailable at this time in region <Region>'.
rootCause: The target region is temporarily running out of capacity in AKS underlays. Regions go through Green/Yellow/Orange/Red phases based on load. Non-EA subscriptions (Free/Azure Plan) are blocked first. QatarCentral is extremely constrained until 2026.
solution: Primary: Use another region (https://aka.ms/aks/regions). Additional: retry later (capacity reclaimed when clusters stop/delete), try different cluster type (public vs private may use different underlay), use EA subscription. For QatarCentral whitelisting, create ICM to Annie Bubinski with subscription IDs and cluster count needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Create/AKSCapacityError or AKSCapacityHeavyUsage Cluster create blocked in constrained region

symptom: AKS nodepool creation or scaling fails with error: ConfigurationVersion 'v0.xxxxxxxx.x' is not supported for AgentBaker. Persists even with latest NodeImageVersion, across Portal/CLI/ARM/Terraform.
rootCause: Internal AgentBaker ConfigurationVersion stored with the agent pool profile has been retired from service. AgentBaker validates configurationVersion before evaluating node image. Common in long-lived clusters, pools from snapshots/security VHDs, or clusters without control plane upgrade for a long time. Not a VM image issue.
solution: Preferred: Upgrade AKS control plane version to regenerate agent pool profiles and rebind to supported AgentBaker configurationVersion. Alternative: Run 'az aks nodepool update --resource-group <rg> --cluster-name <cluster> --name <existing-pool>' on a healthy pool. If all pools fail, create ICM to EEE/AKS RP for service-side reconciliation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Create/AgentBaker NodePool ConfigVersionError

symptom: Creating AKS nodepool with VM size Standard_E112ibds_v5 fails with error 'InvalidParameter: DiskControllerTypeMismatchBetweenOsAndVMScaleSetSize'. VMSS cannot boot with OS image.
rootCause: Standard_E112ibds_v5 only supports NVMe disk controller type, but the OS image from PIR does not specify any diskcontrollerType, so it defaults to SCSI. The mismatch between VM size (NVMe-only) and OS image (SCSI default) causes CRP to reject the request.
solution: Use Standard_E112iads_v5 instead (supports both SCSI and NVMe, less restrictive). Standard_E112ibds_v5 requires NVMe for Gen2 per Azure VM docs. Check SKU API at https://aka.ms/azure-compute-skus for supported disk controller types.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Create/DiskControllerTypeMismatchBetweenOsAndVMScaleSetSize_with_size_Standard_E112ibds_v5

symptom: AKS Machine API LIST Machines returns NotFound/GetAgentPool_NotFound error when querying machines in an agent pool
rootCause: Agent pool is not registered in the AKS database yet, or the target URI has an incorrect agentpool name as path parameter
solution: Verify the agent pool exists by checking the List AgentPools API response or doing a GET AgentPool call. Ensure the correct agentpool name is used in the request URI.

rootCause: Specific REST API version used in Machine API implementation is not supported in the target region
solution: No mitigation available from CSS. Notify the AKS dev team immediately about the unsupported API version in the affected region.

symptom: AKS cluster/nodepool CRUD operations fail with FailedToCreateOrUpdateVMSS or FailToDeleteVMSS with subcode NetworkingInternalOperationError: An unexpected error occurred while processing the network profile of the VM
rootCause: Failures in updating VMSS underlying Azure Compute Fabric or Network resources. Related error codes: RnmServiceDeletionCleanupTimeout, NrpInternalException, InternalServerError.
solution: For delete operations: customer can try manually deleting the underlying VMSS via az vmss delete. The issue may auto-resolve, so retrying may help. Otherwise, engage VM/VMSS team via collaboration task or EEE AzureRT team via IcM with VMSS resource ID and failed CRP operation ID.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/AKS CRUD operation failing with NetworkingInternalOperationError

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with DeleteResourceGroupError/ResourceGroupDeletionTimeout because NSG deletion fails with Microsoft.WindowsAzure.Networking.Rnm.Contracts.Faults
rootCause: Backend services (ARM and NRP) became out-of-sync, causing a leaked NSG object. One service reports the object absent while another reports a to-be-deleted object blocking new objects. Distributed systems consistency issue.
solution: No customer-ready mitigation. Escalate via IcM to EEE Cloudnet/NRP team with all troubleshooting details (AKS RP operation ID, ARM correlation ID, NRP logs). Reference ICM 339177854.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/AKS cluster cannot be deleted because NSG deletion fails with Microsoft.WindowsAzure.Networking.Rnm.Contracts.Faults

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails because managed resources (e.g. Public IP, Load Balancer) cannot be deleted - resource exists in NRP but not in ARM cache, error PublicIPAddressCannotBeDeleted or similar
rootCause: ARM cache sync bug: fast PUT on a network object after a previous DELETE causes ARM to honor the delete but NRP/NFVRP retains the resource, causing ARM-NRP state mismatch. Tracked via Bug 7832895.
solution: CSS engineers use Jarvis ARM resource sync action to recreate the missing resource in ARM (Get resource action then ARM sync resource state action), then customer retries cluster delete. Long-term fix: ARM migration to CosmosDB (PBI 6463105).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/AKS delete errors due to ARM cache issues

symptom: AKS node pool deletion fails with Cannot evict pod as it would violate the pod disruption budget / Too Many Requests error during node drain
rootCause: Pod Disruption Budget (PDB) configured with minAvailable equal to total healthy replicas, or only one healthy pod exists, resulting in disruptionsAllowed=0. AKS cannot evict protected pods during node drain.
solution: Use az aks nodepool delete --resource-group <RG> --cluster-name <Cluster> --name <pool> --ignore-pdb to force delete bypassing PDB constraints. Alternatively, customer adjusts PDB configuration to allow disruptions before retrying.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/AgentPool delete with ignore PDB

symptom: AKS agentpool delete fails with FailToDeleteVMSS/DiskServiceInternalError: The operation is not permitted because there is a lease on the blob, targeting an OS disk
rootCause: Known CRP issue - race condition while processing a VM disk lease during VMSS deletion. Tracked via ADO work item 78300.
solution: Requires manual mitigation from VMSS team. Engage VM/VMSS delivery pod via collaboration task or PG team AzureRT/ScaleSet Service/VMSS via ICM. Provide VMSS ID, CRP operation ID, and CRP operation correlation ID.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Agentpool delete operation failed with FailToDeleteVMSS DiskServiceInternalError

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with DNCCleanupServiceError/UnexpectedStatusFromDNCCleanup: expected dnccleanup to return a 404, got back 202 instead
rootCause: DNC (Delegated Network Controller) cleanup service returns unexpected HTTP 202 status code instead of expected 404 during cluster deletion
solution: Open ICM to PG team. Reference internal DNC cleanup TSG at eng.ms DNC cleanup documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Cluster delete operation failed with DNCCleanupServiceError

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked or ResourceGroupDeletionTimeout - managed resource in Node Resource Group cannot be deleted due to reference from non-managed resource (custom NSG, Private Link Service, route table, IP address)
rootCause: Customer or external services created references/associations between managed resources in the Node Resource Group and non-managed resources, blocking automated cleanup during cluster deletion
solution: Ask customer to: 1) identify blocking resource reference using ASI AKS Operation logs, 2) remove the association/reference to non-managed resources (detach NSG, remove PLS reference, etc.), 3) retry cluster deletion. Note: manually modifying managed resources or adding non-managed resources in the managed RG is not supported.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Cluster delete operation failed with ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked ResourceGroupDeletionTimeout

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked/Timeout due to internal CRP/NRP error when deleting managed VMSS or network resource (e.g. GCM.ContractException, NetworkingInternalOperationError)
rootCause: Internal Azure Compute (CRP) or Network (NRP) errors prevent deletion of managed infrastructure resources during node resource group cleanup
solution: Try manually deleting the managed resource (e.g. az vmss delete). If issue persists, use CRP/NRP operation IDs from troubleshooting to engage owning pod: VM/VMSS pod wiki or Networking pod wiki. Escalate via collaboration task or IcM to EEE AzureRT (compute) or EEE Cloudnet (network).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Cluster delete operation failed with ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked ResourceGroupDeletionTimeout

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked/NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics - network profile vk-* still in use with container nics (Virtual Kubelet/ACI)
rootCause: ACI network profile container NICs not cleaned up properly after Virtual Nodes/ACI removal. Known bug (ADO work item 14104, fix PR 4202309) with possible regression.
solution: No customer-ready mitigation. Escalate via IcM to Support / EEE ACI team with troubleshooting details including AKS operation ID, ARM correlation ID, and NRP HTTP 400 errors against the vk-* network profile.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Cluster delete with NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics

symptom: Unable to delete subnet or parent VNet after AKS cluster deletion - subnet still delegated to Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters with ServiceAssociationLink (SAL) remaining
rootCause: When AKS cluster using Azure CNI Overlay or Pod Subnet networking is deleted, the ServiceAssociationLink (SAL) created during cluster provisioning is not automatically removed
solution: 1) Remove delegation via Portal: Virtual Networks > Subnets > select subnet > Delegate subnet to service > None > Save. 2) Or via CLI: az network vnet subnet update --resource-group <RG> --name <subnet> --vnet-name <vnet> --remove delegations. 3) If above fails, use Jarvis actions for undelegation (note: restricted in China/21V, CSS cannot perform Jarvis undelegation in China).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Undelegate Subnet

rootCause: Agentpool is not registered in the AKS DB yet, or the target URI contains an incorrect agentpool name as path parameter.
solution: Verify the agentpool exists by checking List AgentPools API response or doing GET Agentpool. Correct the agentpool name in the request URI if needed.

symptom: AKS cluster/nodepool CRUD operations fail with FailedToCreateOrUpdateVMSS or FailToDeleteVMSS and sub-code NetworkingInternalOperationError: 'An unexpected error occurred while processing the network profile of the VM'
rootCause: Failures in updating the VMSS underlying Fabric or Network resources in Azure Compute. Related error codes include RnmServiceDeletionCleanupTimeout, NrpInternalException, InternalServerError.
solution: For delete scenarios: customer can try manually deleting the underlying VMSS resource (az vmss delete). Issue may auto-resolve after retry. If not, use ASI to get VMSS resource ID and failed CRP operation ID, then engage VM/VMSS pod team via collaboration task or EEE AzureRT team via IcM. See aka.ms/CCSupNetworkingInternalError.

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with DeleteResourceGroupError and inner error Microsoft.WindowsAzure.Networking.Rnm.Contracts.Faults - NSG (aks-agentpool-*-nsg) cannot be deleted, causing ResourceGroupDeletionTimeout
rootCause: Backend services (NRP and ARM) became out-of-sync: one service reports absence of the NSG while the other reports a to-be-deleted object blocking new operations. This is a leaked object caused by temporary outage or high load in distributed backend services.
solution: No customer-ready mitigation. Escalate via IcM to Cloudnet/NRP (EEE). Gather evidence: 1) Query AKS RP AsyncQoSEvents for failed delete operationID, 2) Query FrontEndContextActivity for full error, 3) Attempt manual NSG delete to get error in ARM logs, 4) Query NRP FrontendOperationEtwEvent with correlationID.

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with PublicIPAddressCannotBeDeleted - public IP still allocated to a load balancer that was deleted from ARM perspective but not from NRP
rootCause: ARM cache out-of-sync bug: resource deleted from ARM but delete did not complete at NRP level. Triggered by fast PUT after DELETE causing ARM to honor stale delete. Tracked in Bug 7832895.
solution: Use ARM resource sync Jarvis action to recreate the missing resource in ARM: 1) Identify missing resource via Get resource Jarvis action, 2) Run ARM sync resource Jarvis action, 3) Customer retries delete. Long-term fix: ARM migration to CosmosDB (PBI 6463105).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/AKS delete errors due to ARM cache issues

symptom: AKS node pool delete fails with 'Cannot evict pod as it would violate the pod disruption budget' - Too Many Requests error during drain, PDB blocks pod eviction (MinAvailable equals CurrentHealthy, DisruptionsAllowed=0)
rootCause: Pod Disruption Budget (PDB) configured with minAvailable equal to total healthy replicas (or only 1 replica exists), preventing any pod eviction during node drain.
solution: Use --ignore-pdb flag: az aks nodepool delete --resource-group <RG> --cluster-name <Cluster> --name <nodepool> --if-match <etag> --ignore-pdb. This forces drain without honoring PDB constraints. Alternatively, adjust PDB (increase replicas or lower minAvailable) before retrying.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/AgentPool delete with ignore PDB

solution: Requires manual mitigation from VMSS team. Gather CRP operation details (VMSS ID, CRP operation ID, correlation ID) using Kusto on azcrp cluster (ApiQosEvent_nonGet), then engage VM/VMSS pod team via collab task or PG team AzureRT/ScaleSet Service VMSS via ICM.

rootCause: Distributed backend services (ARM, NRP) became out-of-sync. One service reports absence of an object while another reports a to-be-deleted 'leaked' object blocking new operations. Caused by temporary outage or high load causing out-of-order writes.

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails because a resource (e.g. PublicIP, LoadBalancer) was deleted from ARM perspective but still exists in NRP/resource provider. Error: PublicIPAddressCannotBeDeleted, 'still allocated to resource' that no longer exists in ARM.
rootCause: ARM cache bug: fast PUT after DELETE causes ARM to honor the delete and remove the resource from its cache while the resource still exists in NRP/NFVRP. ARM is out of sync with the actual resource provider state.
solution: Use Jarvis ARM resource sync action to recreate the missing resource in ARM. Steps: 1) Identify missing resource from ARM perspective using 'Get resource' Jarvis action. 2) Run 'ARM sync resource' Jarvis action to re-create it in ARM. 3) Customer retries the delete which should now succeed. Long-term: ARM migration to CosmosDB.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/AKS delete errors due to ARM cache issues

symptom: AKS nodepool delete fails with 'Too Many Requests' / 'Cannot evict pod as it would violate the pod's disruption budget'. PDB shows MinAvailable equals CurrentHealthy with 0 disruptions allowed.
rootCause: Pod Disruption Budget (PDB) is configured with minAvailable equal to total healthy replicas (or only one replica exists), leaving DisruptionsAllowed=0. AKS cannot evict pods during node drain, blocking nodepool deletion.
solution: Use --ignore-pdb flag: 'az aks nodepool delete --resource-group MyRG --cluster-name MyCluster --name nodepool1 --if-match etag --ignore-pdb'. This forces node drain without honoring PDB constraints. Customer should accept risk of temporary workload disruption.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/AgentPool delete with ignore PDB

symptom: AKS agentpool delete fails with FailToDeleteVMSS/DiskServiceInternalError: 'The operation is not permitted because there is a lease on the blob' targeting an OS disk.
rootCause: Known CRP-level issue: race condition while processing a VM disk's lease during VMSS deletion. Tracked at AzureIaaSVM workitem 78300.
solution: Requires manual mitigation from VMSS team. Gather CRP failed operation details via Kusto (cluster azcrp, ApiQosEvent_nonGet with DELETE + DiskServiceInternalError). Then engage VM/VMSS pod via collaboration task or AzureRT/ScaleSet Service\VMSS via ICM, providing VMSS ID + CRP operation ID + correlation ID.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Agentpool delete operation failed with FailToDeleteVMSS DiskServiceInternalError

symptom: AKS cluster delete fails with ResourceGroupDeletionBlocked, inner error NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics. Network profile starting with 'vk-' (Virtual Kubelet/ACI) cannot be deleted because container NICs are still bound.
rootCause: ACI container NICs remain bound to the Virtual Kubelet network profile after cluster/virtual node operations. Possible regression of a 2021 bug in ACI (fix PR 4202309).
solution: No customer-ready mitigation. Escalate via ICM to 'Support / EEE ACI'. Provide AKS RP operation logs and ARM correlation ID showing HTTP 400 against Microsoft.Network on the 'vk-' network profile.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Cluster delete with NetworkProfileAlreadyInUseWithContainerNics

symptom: Cannot delete subnet or parent VNet after removing AKS cluster. Error: InUseSubnetCannotBeDeleted, subnet still has ServiceAssociationLink 'AzureKubernetesService' from CNI Overlay or Pod Subnet delegation.
rootCause: When AKS cluster with Azure CNI Overlay or Pod Subnet is deleted, the ServiceAssociationLink (SAL) created by subnet delegation to Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters should be auto-removed but sometimes remains.
solution: 1) Remove delegation via Azure Portal: VNet > Subnets > select subnet > Delegate to 'None' > Save. 2) Via CLI: 'az network vnet subnet update --resource-group RG --name subnet --vnet-name vnet --remove delegations'. 3) If above fails: use Jarvis actions for undelegation (CSS except China has access with normal AKSJIT permissions). Ref: CloudNativeCompute wiki Subnet-Delegation-and-Undelegation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Delete/Undelegate Subnet

symptom: ClusterAutoscalerPodsStuckInPending: cluster autoscaler is stuck, pods remain Pending >30 min. Scale-up fails for VMSS-backed node pools.
rootCause: Quota exceeded for VM family used by the VMSS attached to the cluster autoscaler. Insufficient vCPU quota prevents new VM allocation.
solution: Check CRP logs for QuotaExceeded errors. Verify quota for the VMSS VM family in the subscription. Request quota increase for relevant VM families if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Cluster Autoscaler pods stuck in pending

symptom: ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady during CRUD/Start operations. kube-proxy pods stuck in Pending/ImagePullBackOff. Kubelet logs: x509 certificate signed by unknown authority when pulling images from mcr.microsoft.com.
rootCause: Third-party firewall performing SSL deep packet inspection intercepts and re-signs TLS certificates for container image pulls, causing x509 certificate validation failure on AKS nodes.
solution: Ask customer to disable SSL packet inspection on firewall for traffic to mcr.microsoft.com and container registries, then retry. Verify via SSH/node-shell into affected node checking kubelet/containerd logs for x509 errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady because kube proxy stuck pending

symptom: ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady during CRUD operations. Error mentions pods not in Running status but pod names are user workloads (e.g. 3rd-party metrics-server, custom jobs) deployed in kube-system namespace.
rootCause: AKS RP checks all pods in kube-system namespace to determine control plane readiness. User workload pods in kube-system in Succeeded/Failed/Pending state trigger false-positive ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady errors.
solution: Advise customer to move user workloads out of kube-system namespace per AKS FAQ. Delete or relocate completed Job pods from kube-system. Retry cluster operation after cleanup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady because kube proxy stuck pending

symptom: GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch during nodepool upgrade. Autoscaler deletes surge node while AKS RP upgrade is in progress. Existing nodes upgraded in-place instead of creating new surge nodes. Pod eviction takes 5-10 min per node.
rootCause: Race condition between AKS RP and Cluster Autoscaler. Autoscaler issues forceDelete on surge nodes when RP sets new VMSS capacity. ScaleDownDisabledReasonUpgrade annotation not applied to surge node.
solution: Disable cluster autoscaler before nodepool upgrades (az aks nodepool update --disable-cluster-autoscaler), re-enable after upgrade completes. Known bug: ADO 30155571/31289428.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch_for_upgrade

symptom: Nodepool operations (scale, add disk) fail with MMAExtension error: Enable failed exit code 53, incorrect workspace key. OMSAgent extension failure blocks VMSS operations.
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace key in AKS omsagent-secret does not match actual LAW primary key. Or NSG rules block outbound to Log Analytics agent endpoints.
solution: Compare workspace key: kubectl get secrets omsagent-secret -n kube-system -o yaml, decode base64, compare with LAW Portal Agents Management Primary Key. If keys match, check NSG/firewall for blocked outbound to LA endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Invalid workspace key fails cluster operations

symptom: Newly created nodes not registering with AKS API server. ScaleUpTimedOut after ~15min. VMSS creates VM but node never joins cluster. Persists with CAS disabled and manual scale.
rootCause: vmssCSE (Custom Script Extension) accidentally removed from VMSS. Without CSE, nodes lack bootstrap components and cannot register with K8s API server. Required extensions: vmssCSE, AKSLinuxBilling, AKSLinuxExtension.
solution: Verify all 3 VMSS extensions present. If vmssCSE missing, create new agent pool and migrate workloads (new pool will have correct extensions).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/New nodes are not getting registered with AKS API server

symptom: NAP nodes stuck NotReady in Azure CNI Overlay cluster. NodeNetworkConfig events: primaryIP is nil, Subnet is full. New nodes cannot obtain Pod IPs.
rootCause: Pod CIDR exhaustion in CNI Overlay. Each node pre-allocates /24 block (256 IPs). Small Pod CIDR (e.g. /21=8 nodes max) runs out, new nodes cannot get Pod IPs and remain NotReady.
solution: Expand Pod CIDR without cluster recreation: see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/azure-cni-overlay-pod-expand. After expansion, NotReady nodes auto-recover. azure-cns pods redeploy but existing pods unaffected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node Auto Provision subnet is full and pod cidr exhausted

symptom: NAP/Karpenter shows repeated 'Cant replace with a cheaper node' messages every 10-15 minutes with continuous node churn.
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-nap-cant-replace-cheaper-node.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node Auto Provision unable to replace with a cheaper node

symptom: Cluster or node pool creation fails with SKU Not Found or VM Size Not Available error.
rootCause: Requested VM SKU restricted, unavailable, or not offered in target region/zone due to platform capacity limitations or subscription SKU restrictions.
solution: Check SKU availability: az vm list-skus --location <region> --size <sku>. Check ASC Resource Explorer SKU Restrictions. Use alternative VM size or zone. For quota increase route to SAP: Azure > Subscription management > Compute-VM subscription limit increases.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node scaling fails due to allocation or capacity issues

symptom: AKS node scaling operations fail due to vCPU quota exceeded. Total Regional vCPU or VM family quota limit reached.
rootCause: vCPU quotas (Total Regional + VM family) exceeded. VMSS cannot allocate new instances when either limit is reached.
solution: Customer submits quota increase via Azure Portal (standard VM families often auto-approved). For high-demand sizes, route to SAP: Azure > Subscription management > Compute-VM subscription limit increases. Include Sub ID, Region, SKU, Restriction Type, cores requested. Do NOT transfer to VM/VMSS team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Node scaling fails due to allocation or capacity issues

symptom: AKS operations fail with Unable to get log analytics workspace info ResourceNotFound. Error misleadingly points to monitoring addon but may be Container Insights, OMSAgent, or Azure Defender.
rootCause: Log Analytics Workspace referenced by AKS addon has been deleted or is inaccessible. AKS pre-flight validates LAW existence, blocking all operations when LAW missing.
solution: Identify which component references missing LAW in ASC. For Defender: az aks update --disable-defender then --enable-defender. For monitoring addon: disable via https://aka.ms/aks-disable-monitoring-addon or re-create LAW and update addon config.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Operation fails due to missing Log Analytics workspace

symptom: AKS cluster autoscaler fails to scale up, pods stuck in Pending for 30+ minutes with error ClusterAutoscalerPodsStuckInPending
rootCause: VM quota exceeded for the VM family used by the VMSS attached to the cluster autoscaler, preventing new node creation.
solution: Check CRP logs (azcrp ApiQosEvent_nonGet with userAgent autoscaler) and AKS CCP logs (ControlPlaneEvents with category cluster-autoscaler) for QuotaExceeded. Request quota increase for the relevant VM family.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Cluster Autoscaler pods stuck in pending

symptom: AKS operations fail with ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady/PodInitializing, kube-proxy stuck Pending with ImagePullBackOff, kubelet logs show x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
rootCause: Third-party firewall performing SSL/TLS deep packet inspection between AKS nodes and MCR, causing certificate validation failure when pulling container images.
solution: Ask customer to temporarily disable firewall or third-party SSL inspection device, retry the operation. Verify by SSH into affected node checking kubelet/containerd logs.

symptom: AKS CRUD/Start operations fail with ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady, error references non-AKS pods (custom metrics-server, user jobs) in kube-system namespace not reaching Running state
rootCause: AKS RP checks ALL pods in kube-system namespace during operations. User workloads in Completed/Failed/Pending state cause AKS RP to misinterpret them as control plane addon failures.
solution: Advise customer to move all user workloads out of kube-system namespace per AKS FAQ. Verify failing pods are customer pods by checking CCP logs and pod labels.

rootCause: Race condition between AKS RP and cluster autoscaler: autoscaler forceDeletes surge nodes while RP is upgrading. ScaleDownDisabledReasonUpgrade annotation not applied to surge node. Bug 30155571, Issue 31289428.

rootCause: Requested VM SKU restricted in subscription, not offered in target region/zone, or has zonal capacity limitations.
solution: 1) az vm list-skus to check availability. 2) ASC Resource Explorer > SKU Restrictions. 3) Request quota increase or select alternative VM. 4) Transfer to SAP Compute-VM subscription limit increases if needed.

rootCause: Subscription vCPU quota exhausted. Azure two-tier quota: VM-family-specific + total regional vCPU limit.
solution: 1) Submit quota increase via Portal (auto-approved for standard families). 2) Transfer to SAP Compute-VM subscription limit increases. Include: Sub ID, Region, SKU, Restriction Type, cores. Do NOT transfer to VM/VMSS team.

symptom: AKS operations fail with ResourceNotFound: Unable to get log analytics workspace info. Error misleadingly references monitoring addon but may be Azure Defender or Container Insights.
rootCause: LAW referenced by monitoring addon, OMSAgent, or Azure Defender has been deleted or is inaccessible.
solution: 1) In ASC, identify which component uses the LAW. 2) Defender-linked: az aks update --disable-defender then --enable-defender. 3) OMSAgent-linked: disable monitoring addon, re-enable with valid LAW. 4) Custom LAW: use --defender-config with logAnalyticsWorkspaceResourceID.

symptom: ClusterAutoscalerPodsStuckInPending error: cluster autoscaler is stuck, pods remain Pending for >30 minutes due to scale-up failure
rootCause: Quota exceeded for VMs attached to the VMSS used by cluster autoscaler. Insufficient vCPU quota prevents VMSS from scaling up new nodes.
solution: 1) Query ControlPlaneEvents for 'Failed to scale up' or 'QuotaExceeded' using cluster-autoscaler category logs; 2) Check CRP ApiQosEvent_nonGet for autoscaler userAgent to confirm VMSS scale failure; 3) Request quota increase for the relevant VM family in the subscription/region.

symptom: ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady error during CRUD/Start operations: kube-proxy pods stuck in Pending/PodInitializing with ImagePullBackOff, kubelet logs show 'x509: certificate signed by unknown authority' when pulling mcr.microsoft.com images
rootCause: Third-party firewall or network appliance performing SSL/TLS deep packet inspection (DPI) between AKS nodes and MCR, inserting its own certificate. Containerd/kubelet rejects the substituted certificate as untrusted.
solution: 1) SSH/node-shell into the affected node, check kubelet logs for x509 certificate errors; 2) Ask customer to temporarily disable SSL packet inspection on their firewall/proxy for MCR endpoints; 3) Retry the cluster operation after disabling DPI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady because kube proxy stuck pending

symptom: ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady error during CRUD/Start operations: non-AKS user pods (e.g. create-cmr-metrics-server) deployed in kube-system namespace are not in Running state, blocking cluster operations
rootCause: AKS RP validates all pods in kube-system namespace during operations. User workloads deployed in kube-system (especially those with names matching system components like metrics-server) that are in Completed/Failed/Pending state cause validation to fail.
solution: 1) Identify the failing pods in ContextActivity logs (look for non-AKS pod names); 2) Ask customer to move user workloads out of kube-system namespace; 3) Per AKS FAQ, do NOT deploy user workloads in kube-system namespace; 4) Temporarily delete/move problematic pods to unblock operations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/ControlPlaneAddOnsNotReady kube system namespace for user pods

symptom: During nodepool upgrade, autoscaler deletes the surge node causing GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch / AgentCountNotMatch error; existing nodes upgraded in-place instead of creating new surge nodes; pod eviction takes 5-10 minutes per node
rootCause: Race condition between AKS RP and cluster autoscaler. AKS RP failed to annotate the surge node with ScaleDownDisabledReasonUpgrade tag, allowing autoscaler to issue forceDelete on the surge node while upgrade was in progress.
solution: 1) Recommend customer disable cluster autoscaler before performing nodepool upgrades; 2) Re-enable autoscaler after upgrade completes; 3) Bug tracked: ADO work items 30155571 / 31289428; 4) Check AsyncContextActivity for surge node creation and CRP logs for VMSS scale events.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/GetSurgedVms_CountNotMatch_for_upgrade

symptom: AKS nodepool operations (scale, update, add disk) fail with MMAExtension error: Enable failed with exit code 53 - Installation failed due to incorrect workspace key
rootCause: Log Analytics workspace key configured in AKS (stored in omsagent-secret in kube-system) does not match the actual workspace primary key in Azure portal, or the node lacks outbound connectivity to required Log Analytics endpoints.
solution: 1) Get LAW resource ID from az aks show addonProfiles.omsagent; 2) Compare workspace key from kubectl get secrets omsagent-secret -n kube-system -o yaml (base64 decode) with LAW portal Agents Management > Primary Key; 3) If keys mismatch, update the secret; 4) Verify outbound NSG rules allow access to LAW endpoints; 5) Engage Azure Monitor team if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/Invalid workspace key fails cluster operations

symptom: Newly created nodes fail to register with AKS API server; autoscaler scales up VMSS VMs but they never join the cluster; ScaleUpTimedOut warning after 15 minutes; issue persists with manual scale-up
rootCause: VMSS Custom Script Extension (vmssCSE) was accidentally removed from the VMSS. Without CSE, nodes cannot bootstrap essential components and register with the Kubernetes API server.
solution: 1) Verify all 3 required extensions are present on AKS VMSS: vmssCSE, AKSLinuxBilling, AKSLinuxExtension; 2) If vmssCSE is missing, create a new agent pool and migrate workloads; 3) Do not manually remove VMSS extensions from AKS-managed scale sets.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/AKS/TSG/CRUD/Scale/New nodes are not getting registered with AKS API server

symptom: NAP (Node Auto Provision / Karpenter) scaled nodes stuck in NotReady state; NodeNetworkConfig events show 'primaryIP is nil' and 'Subnet is full' errors; NodeClaim status Unknown; azure-cns cannot create network container
rootCause: Pod CIDR exhaustion in Azure CNI Overlay cluster. Each node pre-allocates a /24 block (256 IPs) from Pod CIDR. When Pod CIDR is too small (e.g. /21 supports only 8 nodes), additional nodes cannot obtain IP allocation.
solution: 1) Check NodeNetworkConfig events for 'Subnet is full' and 'primaryIP is nil' errors; 2) Expand Pod CIDR using az aks update per docs (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/azure-cni-overlay-pod-expand); 3) After expansion, NotReady nodes auto-recover; azure-cns pods redeploy but existing pods unaffected.

solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-NAP-cant-replace-cheaper-node.md
